1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128

44	Mathlib's `pullback.lift_fst` / `pullback.lift_snd` are `@[reassoc]` ONLY, not
45	`@[simp]`, so the abbrev `pullback.map _ _ _ _ i₁ i₂ i₃ _ _ ≫ pullback.fst _ _`
46	is not collapsed by `simp` even though the equation holds definitionally. These
47	two local simp lemmas unblock projection-chain collapse for
48	`gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` whose STEP 4 uses
49	`asIso (pullback.map …)`. Candidate for upstream Mathlib contribution. -/
50	
51	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
52	lemma pullback_map_fst_proj {C : Type*} [Category C] {W X Y Z S T : C}
53	    (f₁ : W ⟶ S) (f₂ : X ⟶ S) [Limits.HasPullback f₁ f₂] (g₁ : Y ⟶ T)
54	    (g₂ : Z ⟶ T) [Limits.HasPullback g₁ g₂] (i₁ : W ⟶ Y) (i₂ : X ⟶ Z) (i₃ : S ⟶ T)
55	    (eq₁ : f₁ ≫ i₃ = i₁ ≫ g₁) (eq₂ : f₂ ≫ i₃ = i₂ ≫ g₂) :
56	    Limits.pullback.map f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ i₁ i₂ i₃ eq₁ eq₂ ≫
57	        Limits.pullback.fst g₁ g₂ =
58	      Limits.pullback.fst f₁ f₂ ≫ i₁ :=
59	  Limits.pullback.lift_fst _ _ _
60	
61	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
62	lemma pullback_map_snd_proj {C : Type*} [Category C] {W X Y Z S T : C}
63	    (f₁ : W ⟶ S) (f₂ : X ⟶ S) [Limits.HasPullback f₁ f₂] (g₁ : Y ⟶ T)
64	    (g₂ : Z ⟶ T) [Limits.HasPullback g₁ g₂] (i₁ : W ⟶ Y) (i₂ : X ⟶ Z) (i₃ : S ⟶ T)
65	    (eq₁ : f₁ ≫ i₃ = i₁ ≫ g₁) (eq₂ : f₂ ≫ i₃ = i₂ ≫ g₂) :
66	    Limits.pullback.map f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ i₁ i₂ i₃ eq₁ eq₂ ≫
67	        Limits.pullback.snd g₁ g₂ =
68	      Limits.pullback.snd f₁ f₂ ≫ i₂ :=
69	  Limits.pullback.lift_snd _ _ _

71	/-! ### Chart-bridge: `Proj.awayι ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away _ _))`
72	
73	The helper below is the iter-173 `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe step (a)
74	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). Used by `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (below the `gmScalingP1_cover`
75	definition). -/
76	
77	/-- **`Proj.awayι 𝒜 f _ _ ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 f))`** for any
78	homogeneous element `f` of positive degree.
79	
80	Generic in the element `f` AND in the degree `m` so we can apply it to either
81	`(![X 0, X 1]) i` (the actual chart input from `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover.f i`,
82	which uses `m = (![1, 1]) i`) or `X i` (which uses `m = 1`). A pure rewrite chasing
83	`awayι_toSpecZero` + `Spec.map_comp` + the `algebraKbarAway` defeq. -/
84	private lemma awayι_comp_PLB_hom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
85	    {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m)
86	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar m) :
87	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f hf hm ≫
88	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
89	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar
90	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f))) := by
91	  change Proj.awayι _ _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
92	  rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.awayι_toSpecZero, ← Spec.map_comp,
93	    ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
94	  rfl
95	
96	/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`
97	
98	`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
99	no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
100	the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).
101	
102	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
103	the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
104	the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
105	chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
106	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.
107	
108	The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
109	property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
110	that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/
111	
112	/-- **Chart-1 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
113	coord `u = X 0 / X 1 ↦ u ⊗ λ`, where `λ = X () ∈ GmRing kbar`. Uses
114	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` with the algebra-map `kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing`
115	(target carrier carries `Algebra kbar` because both factors do). Axiom-clean. -/
116	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
117	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
118	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
119	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
120	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))
121	
122	/-- **Chart-0 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
123	coord `t = X 1 / X 0 ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The `λ⁻¹` is `IsLocalization.Away.invSelf (X ())` in
124	`GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`. Axiom-clean. -/
125	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
126	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
127	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
128	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
129	      (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
130	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :
131	        GmRing kbar))
132	
133	/-- **The pullback open cover of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`** along `pullback.fst`, indexed by
134	the 2-chart cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` of `ProjectiveLineBar.left`. The `i`-th
135	component is `pullback (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom) (Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X i) …)`. -/
136	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
137	    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left.OpenCover :=
138	  (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.pullback₁
139	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
140	
141	/-- **The chart-`i` source of `gmScalingP1_cover` is
142	`Spec ((Away 𝒜 ((![X 0, X 1]) i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)`.**
143	
144	Built by composing `pullbackSymmetry`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`, the
145	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` rewrite via `pullback.congrHom`, and `pullbackSpecIso`. Mirrors the
146	Mathlib precedent `OpenCover.pullbackCoverAffineRefinementObjIso`
147	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open:160-166`). Used by `gmScalingP1_chart`.
148	
149	**Uniform-in-`i` (iter-179 refactor `cover-bridge-uniform-i`):** the target type carries
150	`((![X 0, X 1]) i)` rather than `MvPolynomial.X i`. The hoisted helpers
151	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`/`_hm` together with the m-generalised
152	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` keep the bridge chain syntactic so `pullbackSpecIso` applies
153	generically — no `match`-on-`i` decoration. -/
154	private noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
155	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ≅
156	      Spec (CommRingCat.of
157	        (TensorProduct kbar
158	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
159	            ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
160	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))
161	          (GmRing kbar))) :=
162	  pullbackSymmetry _ _ ≪≫
163	    pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
164	    pullback.congrHom
165	      (awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm i)
166	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
167	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)
168	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i))
169	      (show (Gm kbar).hom =
170	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) from rfl) ≪≫
171	    pullbackSpecIso kbar _ (GmRing kbar)
172	
173	/-- **The chart-`i` scheme morphism** `(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme`
174	defining `σ_×` on the `i`-th chart. On chart-1 (target `D₊(X 1)`), the affine coord
175	`u = X 0 / X 1` is sent to `u ⊗ λ`; on chart-0 (target `D₊(X 0)`), `t = X 1 / X 0` is sent
176	to `t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The scheme map is built from `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap` (the chart-side
177	ring maps) via `pullbackSpecIso` + (the chart-ring iso
178	`HomogeneousLocalization.Away ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar`) + `Proj.awayι`.
179	
180	**Status (iter-173):** body landed via the `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe
181	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). The bridge `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (above) identifies the
182	source with `Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing)`. The chart-ring iso
183	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` plus a chart-`i`-specific `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` produces
184	the ring map `Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* Away 𝒜 (X i) ⊗ GmRing`, then `Proj.awayι` lands the
185	result in `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`. -/
186	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
187	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar :=
188	  (gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso kbar i).hom ≫
189	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
190	      ((MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
191	          (fun _ : Unit =>
192	            (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem
193	                (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i)
194	                (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar (otherFin i))) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
195	              (match i with
196	               | ⟨0, _⟩ =>
197	                  (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
198	                    (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) : GmRing kbar)
199	               | ⟨1, _⟩ =>
200	                  algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)
201	                    (MvPolynomial.X ())))).comp
202	        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom)) ≫
203	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
204	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
205	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i) Nat.one_pos

207	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
208	/-- **Shared per-chart helper for `gmScalingP1`** (iter-174 Sub-task A per
209	`analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3). On the `i`-th chart of the
210	`gmScalingP1_cover`, the composition `gmScalingP1_chart kbar i ≫ PLB.hom` agrees with
211	`(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom`.
212	
213	This is the per-chart certificate used by `gmScalingP1_over_coherence` (via
214	`Scheme.Cover.hom_ext` + `Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms_assoc`).
215	
216	**Status (iter-180):** body retired axiom-clean via the empirically-verified
217	`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` recipe of
218	`analogies/pullbackspeciso-bypass.md` (Decision 4). The option collapses the
219	`Algebra.compHom`-driven heartbeat sink on the `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` rewrite
220	that blocked iter-175 through iter-179. -/
221	private lemma gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
222	    gmScalingP1_chart kbar i ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
223	      (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by
224	  -- Stage 1 (iter-179): unfold the chart map, apply `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` after a
225	  -- type-realigning `change`, and collapse the right-hand `Spec.map` chain into
226	  -- `Spec.map (algMap kbar (Away_i ⊗ GmRing))` via
227	  -- `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap` + `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_comp_C`.
228	  unfold gmScalingP1_chart
229	  have h := awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (m := 1) Nat.one_pos
230	    (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
231	    (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
232	  change (gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso kbar i).hom ≫ _ ≫
233	      ((Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
234	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
235	          (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i) Nat.one_pos :
236	        Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
237	          (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (MvPolynomial.X i))) ⟶
238	          Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)) ≫
239	        (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom) = _
240	  rw [h, ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
241	    homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq,
242	    MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_comp_C]
243	  -- Stage 2 (iter-180 fix): the `respectTransparency` option lets the
244	  -- `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` simp lemma fire on the buried iso chain;
245	  -- it produces `pullback.fst _ _ ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar Away_i)` in place
246	  -- of `(pullbackSpecIso).hom ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar (Away_i ⊗ GmRing))`.
247	  -- The follow-up simps collapse the `pullback.congrHom`-wrapped map into a
248	  -- bare `pullback.fst (cover.f i ≫ PLB.hom) Gm.hom`.
249	  unfold gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso
250	  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, Category.assoc, pullbackSpecIso_hom_base,
251	    pullback.congrHom_hom, pullback.lift_fst_assoc, Category.id_comp]
252	  -- Stage 3: re-align `Proj.awayι (![X 0, X 1] i) ⋯ ⋯` with `(cover.openCover.f i)`
253	  -- so `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_hom_fst_assoc` can match its `f' ≫ f` pattern.
254	  change (pullbackSymmetry (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
255	        ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i)).hom ≫
256	      (pullbackRightPullbackFstIso (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom
257	          ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i)).hom ≫
258	        pullback.fst
259	            ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i ≫
260	              (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom)
261	            (Gm kbar).hom ≫
262	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar
263	            (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
264	              ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
265	                Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)))) =
266	        (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom
267	  simp only [pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_hom_fst_assoc,
268	    pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst_assoc]
269	  -- Stage 4: reverse the chart bridge back to `cover.f i ≫ PLB.hom` so the residual
270	  -- matches `(PLB ⊗ Gm).hom`'s definitional form.
271	  rw [← awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm i)

274	    (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i)]
275	  -- Stage 5: expose `(PLB ⊗ Gm).hom = pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom ≫ PLB.hom`
276	  -- and `(gmScalingP1_cover).f i = pullback.fst (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom)
277	  -- ((cover).openCover.f i)`, then close via `pullback.condition_assoc`.
278	  change pullback.snd (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
279	        ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i) ≫
280	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i ≫
281	        (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
282	    pullback.fst (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
283	        ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i) ≫
284	      pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom ≫
285	        (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom
286	  rw [← pullback.condition_assoc]

288	/-- **Intersection-cover X iso** (iter-182 Lane B helper per
289	`analogies/intersection-ring-cross01.md` Decision 3 Recipe 1).
290	
291	Identifies the pullback over the cross chart `(cover.f 0) ⨯ (cover.f 1)` with
292	`Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X 0 * X 1)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing)`, mirroring `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`
293	with the merged generator `X 0 * X 1` of degree 2.
294	
295	The body is a chain of iso steps:
296	1. `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso q awayι_1 (cover.f 0)`: paste the outer pullback
297	   into a left-side composition `pullback (cover.f 0 ≫ q) awayι_1`.
298	2. `pullback.congrHom pullback.condition rfl`: rewrite `cover.f 0 ≫ q` as
299	   `pullback.snd q awayι_0 ≫ awayι_0`.
300	3. `(pullbackRightPullbackFstIso awayι_0 awayι_1 (pullback.snd q awayι_0)).symm`:
301	   bring the outer pullback into `pullback (pullback.snd q awayι_0) (pullback.fst awayι_0 awayι_1)`.
302	4. `pullback.map` with `i₂ = (Proj.pullbackAwayιIso ...).hom`: replace the inner
303	   pullback `pullback awayι_0 awayι_1 ≅ Spec (Away (X 0 * X 1))` (via
304	   `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso`), with the new "fst" being
305	   `Spec.map (awayMap (X 1 hom) rfl)` (via `pullbackAwayιIso_hom_SpecMap_awayMap_left`).
306	5. `pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso q awayι_0 (Spec.map (awayMap _ _))`: collapse the
307	   outer pullback to `pullback q (Spec.map (awayMap _ _) ≫ awayι_0)`.
308	6. `pullback.congrHom rfl (Proj.SpecMap_awayMap_awayι ...)`: identify
309	   `Spec.map (awayMap _ _) ≫ awayι_0 = awayι_(X_0 * X_1)`.
310	7. Apply the standard recipe (mirror of `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`) at the merged
311	   generator `X 0 * X 1` of degree 2: `pullbackSymmetry`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`,
312	   `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` (m = 2), `pullbackSpecIso`. -/
313	private noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso
314	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
315	    pullback ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (0 : Fin 2))
316	        ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (1 : Fin 2)) ≅
317	      Spec (CommRingCat.of
318	        (TensorProduct kbar
319	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
320	            ((MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) * MvPolynomial.X 1))
321	          (GmRing kbar))) := by
322	  refine pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
323	    pullback.congrHom pullback.condition rfl ≪≫
324	    (pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _).symm ≪≫ ?_
325	  refine asIso (pullback.map _ _ _ _ (𝟙 _)
326	    (Proj.pullbackAwayιIso (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
327	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 0)
328	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 0)
329	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)
330	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1)
331	      rfl).hom (𝟙 _)
332	    (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp])
333	    (by
334	      rw [Category.comp_id]
335	      exact (Proj.pullbackAwayιIso_hom_SpecMap_awayMap_left
336	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) _ _ _ _ _).symm)) ≪≫ ?_
337	  refine pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso _ _ _ ≪≫ ?_
338	  refine pullback.congrHom rfl
339	    (Proj.SpecMap_awayMap_awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
340	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 0)
341	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 0)
342	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1) rfl) ≪≫ ?_
343	  exact pullbackSymmetry _ _ ≪≫
344	    pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
345	    pullback.congrHom
346	      (awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (m := 2) (by norm_num)
347	        ((MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) * MvPolynomial.X 1)
348	        ((MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 0).mul (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1)))
349	      (show (Gm kbar).hom =
350	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) from rfl) ≪≫
351	    pullbackSpecIso kbar _ (GmRing kbar)
352	
353	/-- **The substantive `(0, 1)` cross case of `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement`** (iter-181
354	Lane B helper).
355	
356	This is the *single* non-trivial ring-level identity for the cocycle:
357	
358	`pullback.fst ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≫ chart 0 =
359	   pullback.snd ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≫ chart 1`.
360	
361	On the basic-open intersection `D₊(X 0 · X 1) ⊆ ProjectiveLineBarScheme` both chart
362	coordinates are units (chart-0 coord `t = X 1 / X 0`, chart-1 coord `u = X 0 / X 1`,
363	with `t · u = 1`). Under `σ_×([X 0 : X 1], λ) = [λ · X 0 : X 1]` the two chart maps
364	satisfy on the intersection:
365	
366	* chart-0: `t ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹` (i.e. `t/λ`)
367	* chart-1: `u ↦ u ⊗ λ` (i.e. `λ · u`)
368	
369	Substituting `u = 1/t` on the chart-1 side gives `λ · u = λ · (1/t) = λ/t`, and on the
370	chart-0 side `t/λ` corresponds to `u' = 1/(t/λ) = λ/t` under the `u ↔ 1/t` change of
371	coords. Hence the ring-level identity is `λ · u = (1/t) · λ` in
372	`Localization.Away t ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar`, which after multiplying both sides by `t`
373	reduces to `λ · u · t = λ`, true because `u · t = 1`.

524	    ∀ x y : (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).I₀,
525	      pullback.fst ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f x) ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f y) ≫

687	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :

696	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)) := by
697	      refine Function.Injective.isDomain
698	        (algebraMap
699	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
700	            ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
701	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))
702	          (Localization.Away
703	            ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
704	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))) ?_
705	      intro x y h

12	## Question
13	
14	Joint consult for Lane B's 5-iter CHURNING route. After applying
15	`cancel_epi (gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso kbar).inv` (iter-183),
16	the cocycle goal `pullback.fst ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≫ chart 0 =
17	pullback.snd … ≫ chart 1` lifts to a morphism equality over
18	`Spec ((Away (X 0 · X 1)) ⊗ GmRing)`. The substantive residual is to
19	collapse `iso.inv ≫ pullback.fst/snd` through the iso's 7-step
20	`Iso.trans`-chain and reduce to a `Spec.map` of a single ring map. 5
21	iter-183 attempts failed because the chain's STEP 4 uses
22	`asIso (pullback.map _ _ _ _ (𝟙) (Proj.pullbackAwayιIso _).hom (𝟙) _ _)`
23	and the `(asIso …).inv ≫ pullback.fst/snd` collapse step is not
24	served by any Mathlib simp lemma.

45	## Decisions identified
46	
47	### Decision 1: `pullback.map ≫ pullback.fst/snd` HAS NO Mathlib simp lemma — project must build one
48	
49	- **Mathlib state**:
50	  - `pullback.map` is an `abbrev` for `pullback.lift (fst ≫ i₁) (snd ≫ i₂) _`
51	    (`HasPullback.lean:267-271`).
52	  - `pullback.lift_fst` / `pullback.lift_snd` are `@[reassoc]` ONLY —
53	    NOT `@[simp]` (`HasPullback.lean:173-181`).
54	  - Consequence: `simp` does NOT collapse
55	    `pullback.map _ _ _ _ i₁ i₂ i₃ _ _ ≫ pullback.fst _ _` to
56	    `pullback.fst _ _ ≫ i₁`, even though the equation holds by
57	    `pullback.lift_fst` after unfolding `pullback.map`.
58	- **What Mathlib DOES ship at simp severity**:
59	  - `pullback.congrHom_inv` (`@[simp]`, `HasPullback.lean:354-359`) —
60	    unfolds `(pullback.congrHom h₁ h₂).inv` to
61	    `pullback.map _ _ _ _ (𝟙) (𝟙) (𝟙) _ _`.
62	  - `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_inv_fst`,
63	    `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_inv_snd_fst`,
64	    `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_inv_snd_snd`
65	    (`@[reassoc (attr := simp)]`, `Pasting.lean:470-487`).
66	  - `pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso_inv_fst`, `_inv_snd_snd`,
67	    `_inv_fst_snd` (`Pasting.lean:536-552`).
68	  - `pullbackSymmetry_inv_comp_fst`, `_inv_comp_snd`
69	    (`HasPullback.lean:508-514`).
70	  - `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst`, `_inv_snd`
71	    (`@[reassoc (attr := simp)]`,
72	    `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:291-301`).
73	- **Project's current path**: iter-183 attempt 1 used a `simp only`
74	  set that included the `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_inv_*`
75	  simp lemmas. The set did NOT include `Iso.inv_comp_eq` + `asIso_hom`
76	  + the missing `pullback.lift_fst/snd` triple needed to collapse
77	  the `asIso (pullback.map …)` step.
78	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. Mathlib has every iso-step's
79	  inv-fst/snd projection AS A SIMP LEMMA except the `pullback.map`
80	  one. The project hits the gap because its iso chain's STEP 4 uses
81	  `asIso (pullback.map …)` for the inner `pullbackAwayιIso` lift,
82	  which has no Mathlib simp coverage.
83	- **Cost of current divergence**: 5 iters of CHURNING (iter-179 →
84	  iter-183) on this single iso projection. iter-183 progress-critic
85	  triggered mandatory analyst consult.
86	- **Verdict**: **BUILD_PROJECT_HELPER** + iter-184 recipe.
87	  Add two project-side `@[reassoc (attr := simp)]` lemmas
88	  `pullback_map_fst` / `pullback_map_snd` (4 LOC each, body is
89	  `pullback.lift_fst _ _ _` / `pullback.lift_snd _ _ _`). With these
90	  in the local simp set, the iso chain collapses cleanly. This is a
91	  candidate for upstream Mathlib contribution (the `pullback.map`
92	  abbrev SHOULD have its own `≫ fst/snd` simp lemma; project
93	  contribution can stay local-only).

95	### Decision 2: `@[simps]` on the iso would NOT help — chain projections need explicit named lemmas
96	
97	- **Mathlib idiom**: `@[simps! hom inv]` on an `Iso`-valued `def`
98	  generates `_hom` and `_inv` field-projection lemmas at the iso's
99	  STRUCTURE level. For an iso defined as `≪≫`-chain, the generated
100	  lemmas merely UNFOLD the chain — they do NOT pre-compute the
101	  inv-fst/snd projections through the chain.
102	- **Project's current path**: the iter-182 task_result's open
103	  question 2 asked whether `@[simps]` on the iso would auto-generate
104	  the projection lemmas.
105	- **Gap**: divergent-and-wrong (the `@[simps]` route would not
106	  help; iter-183 attempt 1 effectively tried the unfolded form).
107	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** (do not add `@[simps]` to the iso).
108	  Add EXPLICIT named projection lemmas:
109	  ```lean

113	      (gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso kbar).inv ≫
114	        pullback.fst ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f 0)
115	                     ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f 1) =
116	      <explicit Spec.map composition>

122	        pullback.snd ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f 0)
123	                     ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f 1) =
124	      <explicit Spec.map composition>

129	### Decision 3: the "shared target" is the EXISTING `Spec ((Away (X 0 · X 1)) ⊗ GmRing)` — NO new tensor-of-algebras helper
130	
131	- **Mathlib idiom**: `Algebra.TensorProduct.map` exists
132	  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.Basic`) and would produce
133	  `(Away (X 0)) ⊗ GmRing →ₐ (Away (X 0 · X 1)) ⊗ GmRing` from
134	  `awayMap X1_deg rfl ⊗ AlgHom.id`. But this is NOT needed for the
135	  cocycle.
136	- **What's actually needed**: both `iso.inv ≫ pullback.fst ≫ chart 0`
137	  and `iso.inv ≫ pullback.snd ≫ chart 1` are morphisms
138	  `Spec ((Away (X 0 · X 1)) ⊗ GmRing) ⟶ Proj 𝒜`. They factor through
139	  the open immersion `Proj.awayι (X 0 · X 1)` (= mono). After
140	  applying `Proj.SpecMap_awayMap_awayι.symm` to each side to rewrite
141	  `Spec.map (rm_i) ≫ awayι (X i) = Spec.map (rm_i ≫ awayMap X_{1-i} rfl)
142	  ≫ awayι (X 0 · X 1)`, cancel_mono `awayι (X 0 · X 1)` reduces to
143	  ring-level equality.
144	- **Gap**: divergent-and-wrong (the iter-183 attempt 5 explored a
145	  new tensor-of-algebras construction; was reverted due to
146	  `Classical.choice` body pattern).
147	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** — keep the existing `Spec ((Away (X 0 · X 1))
148	  ⊗ GmRing)` target. Reduce to ring algebra via
149	  `SpecMap_awayMap_awayι.symm` + cancel_mono + `algHom_ext` +
150	  `Away.isLocalization_mul` + `t · u = 1`.

171	### Probe 1: `pullback.lift_fst` is NOT a simp lemma
172	
173	```lean
174	@[reassoc]                  -- iter-184 confirmed: only `@[reassoc]`, no simp
175	theorem pullback.lift_fst {W X Y Z : C} {f : X ⟶ Z} {g : Y ⟶ Z}
176	    [HasPullback f g] (h : W ⟶ X) (k : W ⟶ Y) (w : h ≫ f = k ≫ g) :
177	    pullback.lift h k w ≫ pullback.fst f g = h := limit.lift_π _ _
178	```

180	### Probe 2: the 4-lemma `rw` recipe closes the `asIso (pullback.map …)` step
181	
182	```lean
183	example {C : Type*} [Category C] {W X Y S : C}
184	    (f₁ : W ⟶ S) (f₂ : X ⟶ S) [HasPullback f₁ f₂]
185	    (g₂ : Y ⟶ S) [HasPullback f₁ g₂]
186	    (i₂ : X ≅ Y) (eq₁ : f₁ ≫ 𝟙 _ = 𝟙 _ ≫ f₁) (eq₂ : f₂ ≫ 𝟙 _ = i₂.hom ≫ g₂) :
187	    (asIso (pullback.map f₁ f₂ f₁ g₂ (𝟙 _) i₂.hom (𝟙 _) eq₁ eq₂)).inv ≫
188	        pullback.fst f₁ f₂ =
189	      pullback.fst f₁ g₂ := by

271	After Recipe 1's helpers are added, prove the two iso projection lemmas:
272	
273	```lean
274	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
275	private lemma gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso_inv_fst
276	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
277	    (gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso kbar).inv ≫
278	      pullback.fst ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (0 : Fin 2))
279	                   ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (1 : Fin 2)) =

281	    -- `Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.map ...))`
282	    -- but the EXACT form falls out of the simp once the chain collapses;
283	    -- the prover should `change ?goal` AFTER running the simp body to
284	    -- see the precise form, then transcribe it.
285	    sorry := by
286	  unfold gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso
287	  simp only [Iso.trans_inv, Iso.symm_inv, Category.assoc,
288	    pullbackSymmetry_inv_comp_fst, pullbackSymmetry_inv_comp_snd,
289	    pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst, pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_snd,
290	    pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_inv_fst, pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_inv_snd_fst,
291	    pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_inv_snd_snd,
292	    pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_hom_fst, pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_hom_snd,
293	    pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso_inv_fst, pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso_inv_snd_snd,
294	    pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso_inv_fst_snd,
295	    pullback.congrHom_inv, pullback.congrHom_hom,
296	    pullback_map_fst_proj, pullback_map_snd_proj,  -- Recipe 1 helpers
297	    pullback.lift_fst, pullback.lift_snd,           -- belt + braces
298	    Proj.pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst, Proj.pullbackAwayιIso_inv_snd,
299	    Proj.pullbackAwayιIso_hom_SpecMap_awayMap_left,
300	    Proj.pullbackAwayιIso_hom_SpecMap_awayMap_right,
301	    Iso.inv_comp_eq, asIso_hom, asIso_inv,
302	    Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id]
303	  -- The simp pass should close most of the chain; the residual should
304	  -- be a Spec.map equality that you transcribe into the `sorry` RHS above.
305	  rfl  -- or whatever closes the residual
306	```

308	(Mirror for `_inv_snd`.)

310	**Prover strategy**: write the lemma with `RHS = ?_` (named hole), run the
311	simp body, then read the goal and transcribe the residual into the RHS.
312	This avoids the iter-183 attempt 5 "Classical.choice" pattern because the
313	RHS is now COMPUTED by simp, not GUESSED.

315	### Recipe 3 (cocycle closure)
316	
317	With the two projection lemmas in hand, the cocycle proof becomes ~20 LOC:
318	
319	```lean
320	private lemma gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01 (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
321	    pullback.fst ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (0 : Fin 2))
322	        ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (1 : Fin 2)) ≫
323	      gmScalingP1_chart kbar (0 : Fin 2) =
324	    pullback.snd ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (0 : Fin 2))
325	        ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (1 : Fin 2)) ≫
326	      gmScalingP1_chart kbar (1 : Fin 2) := by
327	  apply (cancel_epi (gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso kbar).inv).mp
328	  -- Both sides now have shape `iso.inv ≫ pullback.fst/snd ≫ chart i`.
329	  -- Apply the projection lemmas to collapse `iso.inv ≫ pullback.fst/snd`.
330	  simp only [← Category.assoc,
331	    gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso_inv_fst,
332	    gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso_inv_snd]
333	  -- Goal: `Spec.map (RHS_fst) ≫ chart 0 = Spec.map (RHS_snd) ≫ chart 1`.
334	  -- Unfold `chart i = iso_i.hom ≫ Spec.map (rm_i) ≫ awayι (X i)`.
335	  unfold gmScalingP1_chart
336	  -- Collapse Spec.map and awayι using SpecMap_awayMap_awayι:
337	  --   awayι (X i) = Spec.map (awayMap X_{1-i} rfl) ≫ awayι (X 0 · X 1)
338	  -- so both sides factor through awayι (X 0 · X 1).
339	  -- After cancel_mono awayι (X 0 · X 1), reduce to Spec.map equality.
340	  rw [← Proj.SpecMap_awayMap_awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
341	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 0)
342	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 0)
343	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1) rfl,
344	      ← Proj.SpecMap_awayMap_awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
345	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)
346	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1)
347	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 0) (mul_comm _ _)]

1	\chapter{Abelian-variety rigidity: morphisms from a genus-\(0\) curve into an abelian variety are constant}
2	\label{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}
3	label{chap:avr_for_rr}
4	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/ChartIso.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean AlgebraicJacobian/RigidityLemma.lean

6	% chapter is the writer's only editable file; flagged in the writer report for promotion
7	% to macros/common.tex if desired. \providecommand is a no-op if it is later added globally.
8	\providecommand{\fatsemi}{\mathbin{\,;\,}}
9	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-157). This chapter is the dedicated home of the project's COMMITTED
10	% genus-0 route (route (c)): the characteristic-free abelian-variety rigidity stack. It is
11	% 1:1 with the new upstream Lean file AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean, which
12	% imports AlgebraicJacobian.Genus and is imported by AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian (breaking the
13	% RigidityKbar -> Rigidity -> Jacobian import cycle so that genusZeroWitness can consume the
14	% keystone directly). The headline prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV is the char-free
15	% replacement (no [CharZero]) for the over-k-bar declaration rigidity_over_kbar of
16	% \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} (which remains in tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and
17	% still carries [CharZero]). The df=0 / H^0(P^1,Omega)=0 / Serre-duality framing is the
18	% fallback (a), NOT the route developed here.
19	The genus-\(0\) arm of the Jacobian-witness construction has a \emph{trivial} witness object
20	(\(J = \Spec k\)); its only remaining mathematical content is a rigidity \emph{statement}:
21	every pointed morphism from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve of genus \(0\)
22	into an abelian variety is the constant morphism at the identity. We prove this
23	\textbf{characteristic-free} --- the protected goal is over an arbitrary field \(k\), so the
24	cheaper differential argument (via $H^0(\mathbb P^1, \Omega) = H^0(\mathbb P^1, \mathcal
25	O(-2)) = 0\(, hence \)df = 0\(, hence \)f$ constant) is unavailable: it breaks on a
26	characteristic-\(p\) Frobenius-descent wall, where a nonconstant \(f\) can have \(df = 0\). The
27	rigidity route below sidesteps that wall.
28	The chain has two parts. The \emph{Rigidity Lemma} (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, Mumford,
29	Form~I; equivalently Milne's Rigidity Theorem~1.1) is an elementary properness/closed-map
30	argument --- it uses neither the theorem of the cube nor any cohomology, is the entry point
31	for formalisation, and is now proven axiom-clean. From the Rigidity Lemma, Milne's \S I.3
32	derives the genus-\(0\) base case \emph{without} the theorem of the cube --- and, in this
33	project's reading, \emph{without} Milne's Theorem~3.2 (rational-map extension), Lemma~3.3,
34	Auslander--Buchsbaum, or differentials (the ``4th route''; see
35	\cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}). The decisive observation is that \(\mathbb P^1\) is
36	\emph{already complete} and \(f \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A\) is \emph{already a total morphism}, so
37	there is no rational defect map to extend over a complete surface and Theorem~3.2 never enters.
38	Instead the base case is closed by the \textbf{\(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling shortcut} (the project's
39	primary route, an Archon-original realisation of Milne Proposition~3.9): one uses the
40	\emph{multiplicative scaling} action $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to
41	\mathbb P^1\(, \)(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x\( (the M\"obius scaling fixing both \)0$ and
42	\(\infty\)), which is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb
43	G_m\( (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1}). Normalising \)f(0) = \eta_A\( and composing \)h := \sigma_\times
44	\fatsemi f\(, one applies additivity of \)\mathrm{Hom}(-, A)$ over a product
45	(\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}, Milne Corollary~1.5) --- whose Lean signature requires
46	only the \emph{first} factor complete --- with \(V = \mathbb P^1\) proper, \(W = \mathbb G_m\), and
47	base points \(0 \in \mathbb P^1\), \(1 \in \mathbb G_m\). Because \(0\) is a \emph{fixed point} of
48	scaling, the \(W\)-axis restriction \(\lambda \mapsto h(0, \lambda) = f(\lambda \cdot 0) = f(0)\) is
49	\emph{constant} at \(\eta_A\), so the decomposition collapses to \(h = p \fatsemi f\), i.e.\
50	\(f(\lambda x) = f(x)\). Specialising \(x = 1\) gives that \(f|_{\mathbb G_m}\) is constant, and since
51	\(\mathbb G_m\) is dense in \(\mathbb P^1\) with \(A\) separated, \(f\) is constant
52	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, via the separated-target/dominant-source handle
53	\cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}). This route uses \emph{only} the proven Corollary~1.5, a
54	scaling fixed point, and density --- it never forms the additive defect map, never invokes
55	\(\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0\), and so sidesteps the ``image of a group homomorphism is a
56	closed subgroup'' input that has no Mathlib support. The classical \(\mathbb G_a\)-additive
57	argument (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial} via the \(\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m\) incompatibility
58	\cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}, the heart of Milne Proposition~3.9) is retained only as the
59	Milne-faithful alternative and is \emph{off} the genus-\(0\) critical path. The corollary that a
60	pointed regular map of abelian
61	varieties is a homomorphism (\cref{lem:av_regular_map_is_hom}, Milne Corollary~1.2) is recorded
62	for completeness and for Route~A but is \emph{not} needed on the genus-\(0\) path; Milne's
63	Theorem~3.2 (\cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}) is likewise demoted to a Route-A-only item
64	(the Albanese universal property), no longer on the genus-\(0\) critical path
65	(\cref{rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap}). Combining these
66	gives that every morphism \(\mathbb P^1 \to A\) is constant
67	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}); composing with the classification of genus-\(0\)
68	curves (\cref{thm:genus_zero_curve_iso_p1}, \(C \cong \mathbb P^1\) over \(\bar k\)) yields the
69	headline \cref{prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}. The theorem of the cube (Milne \S I.5) is
70	\emph{not} used anywhere on this path (see \cref{rmk:cube_not_needed}); the chain invokes
71	neither Serre duality nor the FGA \(\Pic\) representability engine, so the genus-\(0\) arm is
72	fully decoupled from Route~A.
73	\medskip
74	Throughout, \(\bar k\) is an algebraically closed field of arbitrary characteristic. An
75	\emph{abelian variety} over \(\bar k\) is a complete (proper) irreducible group variety; in the
76	project's Lean encoding \(A\) is an object of \(\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)\) carrying
77	\(\mathtt{GrpObj}\), \(\mathtt{IsProper}\), \(\mathtt{Smooth}\), and \(\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}\).
78	\section{The Rigidity Lemma}
79	\begin{lemma}
80	\leanok
81	[Rigidity Lemma (Mumford, Form I)]
82	  \label{thm:rigidity_lemma}
83	  \uses{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}
84	  
85	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_lemma}
86	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
87	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "RIGIDITY LEMMA. (Form I.) Let \(X\) be a complete variety, \(Y\) and \(Z\)
88	  % any varieties, and \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) a morphism such that for some
89	  % \(y_0 \in Y\), \(f(X \times \{y_0\})\) is a single point \(z_0\) of \(Z\). Then there is a
90	  % morphism \(g \colon Y \to Z\) such that if \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is the
91	  % projection, \(f = g \circ p_2\)."
92	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43.}
93	  Let \(X\) be a complete variety, and let \(Y\), \(Z\) be any varieties over \(\bar k\). Let
94	  \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) be a morphism such that for some \(y_0 \in Y\) the image
95	  \(f(X \times \{y_0\})\) is a single point \(z_0 \in Z\). Then there is a morphism
96	  \(g \colon Y \to Z\) with \(f = g \circ p_2\), where \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is the
97	  projection; that is, \(f\) depends only on the \(Y\)-coordinate, and \(g(y) = f(x_0, y)\) for any
98	  fixed \(x_0 \in X\).
99	\end{lemma}
100	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. Choose any point \(x_0 \in X\), and define \(g \colon Y \to Z\) by
101	% \(g(y) = f(x_0, y)\). Since \(X \times Y\) is a variety, to show that \(f = g \circ p_2\), it is
102	% sufficient to show that these morphisms coincide on some open subset of \(X \times Y\). Let
103	% \(U\) be an affine open neighbourhood of \(z_0\) in \(Z\), \(F = Z - U\), and \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(F))\);
104	% then \(G\) is closed in \(Y\) since \(X\) is complete and hence \(p_2\) is a closed map. Further
105	% \(y_0 \notin G\) since \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}\). Therefore \(Y - G = V\) is a non-empty
106	% open subset of \(Y\). For each \(y \in V\), the complete variety \(X \times \{y\}\) gets mapped by
107	% \(f\) into the affine variety \(U\), and hence to a single point of \(U\). But this means that for
108	% any \(x \in X\), \(y \in V\), \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g \circ p_2(x, y)\), and this proves our
109	% assertion."
110	\begin{proof}
111	\leanok
112	% NOTE (iter-159 review): the iter-157 unsoundness REMAINS REPAIRED. The collapse hypothesis
113	% (_hf : f(X x {y0}) = {z0}) is threaded through all three helpers (rigidity_lemma ->
114	% rigidity_core -> rigidity_eqOn_dense_open) and GENUINELY CONSUMED in the body of
115	% rigidity_eqOn_dense_open (proves y0 \notin G, the load-bearing non-emptiness of V := Y - G).
116	% The counterexample f = fst is excluded (under f = fst, _hf forces X to collapse to a point).
117	% rigidity_eqOn_dense_open is sorry-free in its OWN body (hfib, the pullback-fibre fact over the
118	% k-bar-point y0, is closed). rigidity_snd_lift and snd_left_isClosedMap are axiom-clean (no sorryAx).
119	% NOTE (iter-160): the \uses edges run FORWARD along the true Lean dependency order
120	% (rigidity_lemma -> rigidity_eqOn_dense_open -> rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine), so the
121	% not-proven status of the residual sorry propagates up and de-launders BOTH thm:rigidity_lemma and
122	% lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open. No backward edge from the leaf lemmas back up the chain.
123	% NOTE (iter-161): bridge 2 (rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine) was DECOMPOSED into two named
124	% top-level sub-lemmas along the route-B (cohomology-free) split:
125	%   - Step 2, morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints: "two morphisms agreeing at every closed point of a
126	%     reduced Jacobson source into a separated target are equal" -- PROVEN axiom-clean (the
127	%     dense-closed-points coproduct probe + ext_of_isDominant; the connective Mathlib lacks).
128	%   - Step 1, rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine: Mumford's per-closed-slice constancy
129	%     -- the genuinely-deep residual, still `sorry`. THIS is now the chain's lone deep residual
130	%     (bridge 2's body is real assembly: Step 2 proven, the JacobsonSpace U instance now derivable).
131	% SIGNATURE CHANGE (iter-161, plan-authorized): Step 2's globalisation needs the closed points of
132	% the saturated open U to be DENSE, i.e. U Jacobson, which over Spec k-bar follows from (X tensor Y)
133	% being LOCALLY OF FINITE TYPE. So [LocallyOfFiniteType (X tensor Y).hom] is now carried on FIVE
134	% chain lemmas (rigidity_lemma, rigidity_core, rigidity_eqOn_dense_open,
135	% rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine, rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine), in
136	% addition to [IsAlgClosed kbar]. Free downstream: curves and abelian varieties are of finite type
137	% over k-bar. The earlier "[IsAlgClosed] is the only added instance" claim is hereby retired.
138	  Fix any point \(x_0 \in X\) and define \(g \colon Y \to Z\) by \(g(y) = f(x_0, y)\) (the
139	  composite of \(y \mapsto (x_0, y)\) with \(f\)). Since \(X \times Y\) is a variety (in
140	  particular reduced and irreducible, so separated and with no embedded structure), to prove
141	  the equality of morphisms \(f = g \circ p_2\) it suffices to prove they agree on a non-empty
142	  open subset of \(X \times Y\).
143	  Let \(U\) be an affine open neighbourhood of \(z_0\) in \(Z\), put \(F = Z - U\) (a closed set),
144	  and set \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(F))\). Because \(X\) is complete, the projection
145	  \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is a \emph{closed} map (this is the defining property of
146	  completeness/properness); hence \(G\), the image of the closed set \(f^{-1}(F)\), is closed in
147	  \(Y\). The hypothesis \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\} \subseteq U\) gives
148	  \(f^{-1}(F) \cap (X \times \{y_0\}) = \varnothing\), so \(y_0 \notin G\). Therefore
149	  \(V := Y - G\) is a non-empty open subset of \(Y\).
150	  For each \(y \in V\) we have \(X \times \{y\} \cap f^{-1}(F) = \varnothing\), i.e.
151	  \(f(X \times \{y\}) \subseteq U\). Thus the complete (proper) variety \(X \times \{y\}\) is
152	  mapped by \(f\) into the \emph{affine} variety \(U\). A proper connected variety mapping to an
153	  affine variety has image a single point (a global regular function on a complete connected
154	  variety is constant). Hence \(f(X \times \{y\})\) is one point, necessarily
155	  \(f(x_0, y) = g(y)\). So for all \(x \in X\) and \(y \in V\),
156	  \[ f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g(y) = (g \circ p_2)(x, y). \]
157	  The two morphisms \(f\) and \(g \circ p_2\) thus agree on the non-empty open
158	  \(X \times V\), which is dense in the irreducible \(X \times Y\); as \(Z\) is separated they
159	  agree everywhere. \qedhere
160	\end{proof}
161	\begin{remark}
162	  \label{rmk:rigidity_lemma_cube_free}
163	  The proof of \cref{thm:rigidity_lemma} uses \emph{only} (i) that completeness of \(X\) makes
164	  \(p_2\) a closed map, and (ii) that a proper connected variety has no nonconstant map to an
165	  affine variety. It uses neither the theorem of the cube nor any cohomology, and is valid in
166	  arbitrary characteristic. This is the designated formalisation entry point: it is the
167	  lowest-prerequisite link of the chain. (Mumford gives this as the geometric heart of the
168	  ``second proof'' of commutativity of an abelian variety; Milne packages the same statement
169	  as the Rigidity Theorem~1.1, requiring \(V\) complete and \(V \times W\) geometrically
170	  irreducible.)
171	\end{remark}
172	\begin{remark}
173	  \label{rmk:rigidity_lemma_decomposition}
174	  The Lean formalisation of \cref{thm:rigidity_lemma} splits into three helper steps, mirroring
175	  the proof above: \texttt{rigidity\_snd\_lift} (the cartesian-monoidal algebra identifying the
176	  collapsed map \((x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)\) followed by \(f\) with \(g \circ p_2\));
177	  \texttt{rigidity\_core} (the scheme-level gluing: two maps out of the geometrically irreducible
178	  reduced \(X \times Y\) into the separated \(Z\) that agree on a non-empty open subset agree
179	  everywhere, by separatedness); and \texttt{rigidity\_eqOn\_dense\_open}
180	  (\cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}, the genuine geometry: the construction of the non-empty
181	  open \(X \times V\) and the slice-constancy of \(f\) on it). The collapse hypothesis
182	  \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}\) must be threaded through \emph{all three} helpers, since it is
183	  what makes \(V\) non-empty; a decomposition that drops it from the lower helpers is unsound
184	  (see \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}).
185	  \emph{Status (iter-161).} Bridge~1 (the closed-map step) is \textbf{built} as the axiom-clean
186	  helper \texttt{snd\_left\_isClosedMap}. The fibre-over-\(y_0\) fact is \textbf{closed} (via the
187	  coarse \texttt{PullbackCarrier} layer), so \texttt{rigidity\_eqOn\_dense\_open} is
188	  \texttt{sorry}-free in its own body. Bridge~2 (slice-constancy on the saturated open \(U\),
189	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine}) has now been \textbf{decomposed} along its
190	  cohomology-free route-(c) split into two named sub-lemmas: the globalisation
191	  \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints} (Step~2, ``agree at every closed point of a reduced
192	  Jacobson source \(\Rightarrow\) equal'') is \textbf{proven axiom-clean}, and the per-closed-slice
193	  constancy \cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine} (Step~1, Mumford's slice
194	  argument) is the chain's \emph{single genuinely-deep residual} \texttt{sorry}. The body of
195	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine} is therefore real assembly (Step~2 over Step~1).
196	  % NOTE: (iter-162 review) STALE as of iter-162 — Step~1 is now PROVEN and the WHOLE
197	  % Rigidity-Lemma chain (thm:rigidity_lemma … lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine,
198	  % via the new helper lem:isIntegral_of_retract_of_integral) is sorry-free + axiom-clean
199	  % ({propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}, re-verified). The "single genuinely-deep residual
