217	\leanok
218	[Dense-open agreement: the geometric heart of the Rigidity Lemma]
219	  \label{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}
220	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}
221	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
222	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "RIGIDITY LEMMA. (Form I.) Let \(X\) be a complete variety, \(Y\) and \(Z\)
223	  % any varieties, and \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) a morphism such that for some
224	  % \(y_0 \in Y\), \(f(X \times \{y_0\})\) is a single point \(z_0\) of \(Z\). Then there is a
225	  % morphism \(g \colon Y \to Z\) such that if \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is the
226	  % projection, \(f = g \circ p_2\)."
227	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43 (the
228	  dense-open construction is the body of that proof).}
229	  Let \(X\) be a complete variety and \(Y\), \(Z\) any varieties over \(\bar k\). Let
230	  \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) be a morphism, fix a point \(x_0 \in X\), and suppose given
231	  \(y_0 \in Y\), \(z_0 \in Z\) with the \emph{collapse hypothesis}
232	  \[ f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\} \qquad
233	     \text{(in Lean: } \mathtt{lift}\,(\mathbf 1_X)\,(\mathtt{toUnit}\,X \fatsemi y_0) \fatsemi f
234	     = \mathtt{toUnit}\,X \fatsemi z_0\text{).} \]
235	  Then there is a non-empty open subset \(U \subseteq X \times Y\) on which \(f\) agrees, as a
236	  scheme morphism, with the collapsed map \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\), where
237	  $\mathtt{retract} := \mathtt{lift}\,(\mathtt{toUnit}\,(X \times Y) \fatsemi x_0)\,
238	  (p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y)\( is the ``\)(x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)$'' endomorphism. Concretely
239	  \(U = X \times V\) with \(V := Y - G\), \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(Z - U_0))\) for an affine open
240	  neighbourhood \(U_0\) of \(z_0\), and on \(U\) one has \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y)\).
241	  \medskip
242	  \noindent\textbf{The collapse hypothesis is load-bearing.} The non-emptiness of \(V\) is exactly
243	  the assertion \(y_0 \notin G\), and this holds \emph{because} $f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}
244	  \subseteq U_0\( keeps the slice over \)y_0\( inside the affine \)U_0\(, away from \)F = Z - U_0$. A
245	  version of this lemma \emph{without} the collapse hypothesis is false: for \(X = Y = Z\) and
246	  \(f = p_1\) the first projection, \(f\) and \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f \colon (x, y) \mapsto x_0\)
247	  agree on no non-empty open (the agreement locus is contained in \(\{x = x_0\}\), which has empty
248	  interior in the irreducible \(X \times Y\)). Therefore the formal target \emph{must} carry the
249	  collapse hypothesis as an explicit antecedent; dropping it (as an earlier decomposition
250	  silently did) makes the deferred goal unsatisfiable.
251	  \medskip
252	  \noindent\textbf{Formalization note: algebraically closed base, and locally-of-finite-type
253	  source.} The prose already takes \(\bar k\) algebraically closed, but the formal statement must
254	  \emph{encode} two properties. First, the cohomology-free proof of the slice-constancy bridge
255	  (below) needs each per-closed-point residue field \(\kappa(y)\) to be \(\bar k\) itself, so that a
256	  proper integral slice has global sections \(\Gamma = \bar k\) and maps to a single \(\bar k\)-point of
257	  the affine \(U_0\); this requires \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\). Second, the globalisation of the
258	  per-slice agreement (Step~2 of the bridge, \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}) needs the
259	  closed points of the saturated open \(U\) to be \emph{dense} --- i.e.\ \(U\) to be a Jacobson space.
260	  Over the algebraically closed \(\bar k\) this holds precisely because \(X \times Y\) is \emph{locally of
261	  finite type} over \(\bar k\) (\texttt{LocallyOfFiniteType.jacobsonSpace} transports the Jacobson
262	  property of \(\Spec \bar k\), and \texttt{closure\_closedPoints} then gives density); this requires
263	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\). Accordingly the formalization carries
264	  \emph{both} \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\) and
265	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\) on this lemma (in addition to
266	  \([\mathtt{Field}\ \bar k]\)), and propagates the same two instances to \cref{thm:rigidity_lemma},
267	  its core helper \texttt{rigidity\_core}, and the bridge sub-lemmas
268	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine} and
269	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}. Both cost nothing downstream: the
270	  consumers \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant} and \cref{prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} (Lean
271	  \texttt{rigidity\_genus0\_curve\_to\_grpScheme}) \emph{already} assume
272	  \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\) --- the base-field variable is literally named \(\mathtt{kbar}\) ---
273	  and curves and abelian varieties are of finite type over \(\bar k\), so the
274	  locally-of-finite-type instance is automatic there.
275	\end{lemma}
276	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. Choose any point \(x_0 \in X\), and define \(g \colon Y \to Z\) by
277	% \(g(y) = f(x_0, y)\). Since \(X \times Y\) is a variety, to show that \(f = g \circ p_2\), it is
278	% sufficient to show that these morphisms coincide on some open subset of \(X \times Y\). Let
279	% \(U\) be an affine open neighbourhood of \(z_0\) in \(Z\), \(F = Z - U\), and \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(F))\);
280	% then \(G\) is closed in \(Y\) since \(X\) is complete and hence \(p_2\) is a closed map. Further
281	% \(y_0 \notin G\) since \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}\). Therefore \(Y - G = V\) is a non-empty
282	% open subset of \(Y\). For each \(y \in V\), the complete variety \(X \times \{y\}\) gets mapped by
283	% \(f\) into the affine variety \(U\), and hence to a single point of \(U\). But this means that for
284	% any \(x \in X\), \(y \in V\), \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g \circ p_2(x, y)\), and this proves our
285	% assertion."
286	\begin{proof}
287	  \leanok
288	  Let \(U_0\) be an affine open neighbourhood of \(z_0\) in \(Z\), put \(F = Z - U_0\), and set
289	  \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(F))\). Because \(X\) is complete, the projection \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is a
290	  \emph{closed} map, so \(G\) (the image of the closed set \(f^{-1}(F)\)) is closed in \(Y\). The
291	  collapse hypothesis \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\} \subseteq U_0\) gives
292	  \(f^{-1}(F) \cap (X \times \{y_0\}) = \varnothing\), hence \(y_0 \notin G\); this is the only place
293	  the collapse hypothesis is used, and it is essential, for \(V := Y - G\) is non-empty precisely
294	  because \(y_0 \in V\). For each \(y \in V\) the proper variety \(X \times \{y\}\) is mapped by \(f\)
295	  into the affine \(U_0\), hence to a single point, necessarily \(f(x_0, y)\). So for all \(x \in X\),
296	  \(y \in V\), we have \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)(x, y)\), i.e.\ \(f\) and
297	  \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) agree on the non-empty open \(U := X \times V\). (In the Lean
298	  encoding the two slice-constancy bridges are the closed-map step from properness and the
299	  ``proper-into-affine has single-point image'' step; see the docstring of
300	  \texttt{rigidity\_core}.)
301	  \medskip
302	  \noindent\textbf{Formalization notes (the concrete char-free Mathlib route).} The textbook
303	  proof above is realised in Lean by three pieces. They cite Mathlib lemma names as the chosen
304	  route; none uses cohomology, and the relative Stein-factorisation framing is deliberately
305	  \emph{avoided} (see below).
306	  \emph{Bridge~1 (the closed-map step) --- BUILT.} ``\(X\) complete \(\Rightarrow p_2\) closed'' is
307	  the axiom-clean helper \texttt{snd\_left\_isClosedMap} (iter-158). It is obtained from
308	  \texttt{IsProper.toUniversallyClosed} on \(X\), base-changed across the canonical pullback square
309	  to make \(p_2\) universally closed, using that the monoidal projection \emph{is} the chosen
310	  pullback projection: \texttt{Over.snd\_left} rewrites \((\mathtt{snd}\,X\,Y).\mathtt{left}\) to
311	  \(\mathtt{Limits.pullback.snd}\ X.\mathtt{hom}\ Y.\mathtt{hom}\) exactly. This step is already
312	  proven.
313	  \emph{The fibre fact (non-emptiness of \(V\)) --- RESOLVED route, char-free.} That the slice
314	  \(X \times \{y_0\}\) over the \(\bar k\)-rational point \(y_0\) is exactly the image of the slice
315	  section \(s \colon X \to X \times Y\) is formalised \emph{without} the residue-field /
316	  \texttt{Triplet} / tensor-product machinery (that fine \texttt{carrierEquiv} layer is the wrong
317	  granularity and is to be avoided). One pastes the identity square for the section against the
318	  canonical pullback square (\texttt{IsPullback.of\_right},
319	  \texttt{IsPullback.of\_horiz\_isIso}; the section identities \(s \fatsemi p_1 = \mathbf 1\) and
320	  \(s \fatsemi p_2 = X.\mathtt{hom} \fatsemi y_0\) come from \texttt{lift\_fst}/\texttt{lift\_snd}
321	  with \texttt{Over.w} on the unit) and reads off the fibre with the \emph{coarse} topological
322	  layer of \texttt{PullbackCarrier}, namely
323	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pullback.image\_preimage\_eq\_of\_isPullback}. This needs no
324	  \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}]\).
325	  \emph{Bridge~2 (slice-constancy / the agreement equation) --- RESOLVED route, cohomology-FREE.}
326	  ``Proper connected slice into affine \(\Rightarrow\) single point, glued to a morphism equality on
327	  \(U\)'' is realised by a \textbf{global-sections + per-closed-point} route, \emph{not} by any
328	  relative Stein-factorisation or proper-pushforward \(f_*\mathcal O = \mathcal O\) statement: the
329	  latter is a genuine Mathlib gap (no Stein factorisation, no \(H^0\) base change), and route~(c)
330	  exists precisely to avoid coherent cohomology, so it must \emph{not} be attempted. The concrete
331	  stack is: for each closed point \(y \in V\) one has \(\kappa(y) = \bar k\) (alg-closed, finite type);
332	  the slice \(X_y\) is proper integral, so its global sections form a field
333	  (\texttt{isField\_of\_universallyClosed}) module-finite over \(\bar k\)
334	  (\texttt{finite\_appTop\_of\_universallyClosed}), and algebraic closedness kills the finite
335	  extension to give \(\Gamma(X_y) = \bar k\). A morphism into the affine \(U_0\) is pinned by its ring
336	  map on global sections (\texttt{ext\_of\_isAffine}), so \(f\) and
337	  \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) agree on each closed slice. Closed points are dense in the
338	  locally-of-finite-type \(U\) (\texttt{closure\_closedPoints} / the Jacobson-space property of
339	  locally-of-finite-type \(\bar k\)-schemes; in the formalization this density is supplied by the new
340	  hypothesis \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\)), so the per-slice
341	  agreement globalises to all of \(U\) via the reduced-source / separated-target rigidity
342	  \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant\_of\_isSeparated'} (the same handle \texttt{rigidity\_core} already
343	  uses). The packaged ``morphisms agreeing on a dense set of closed points are equal''
344	  hom-extensionality --- the one connective Mathlib does not supply directly (it gives only the
345	  single-dominant-morphism \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant}) --- has now been built as the axiom-clean
346	  \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints} (Step~2): it assembles the closed points into one
347	  dominant coproduct probe and applies \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant}. The whole argument is extracted
348	  into the dedicated obligation \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine} below, whose body
349	  wires that proven Step~2 over the per-slice Step~1
350	  (\cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}); Step~1 (Mumford's per-closed-slice
351	  constancy) is now the chain's \emph{single} genuinely-deep residual \texttt{sorry}.
352	\end{proof}
353	\begin{lemma}
354	\leanok
355	[Bridge~2: slice-constancy on a saturated open mapping into an affine]
356	  \label{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine}
357	  
358	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine}
359	  % NOTE (iter-161): the iter-160 SIGNATURE GAP is RESOLVED. The Lean proof wires Step 2
360	  % (morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints, PROVEN axiom-clean) over Step 1
361	  % (rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine, the residual sorry). Step 2 needs the closed
362	  % points of U to be DENSE, i.e. U Jacobson; over k̄ this follows from (X⊗Y) being locally of finite
363	  % type, so [LocallyOfFiniteType (X⊗Y).hom] is now a stated hypothesis of this lemma (and across the
364	  % chain). With it, the `JacobsonSpace U` instance is a routine discharge (LocallyOfFiniteType.
365	  % jacobsonSpace + JacobsonSpace.of_isOpenEmbedding), no longer an as-typed gap. The only `sorry`
366	  % reachable from this lemma is now Step 1 (Mumford's per-closed-slice constancy). The hypothesis
367	  % set is recorded in the statement and proof prose below (both [IsAlgClosed k̄] AND
368	  % [LocallyOfFiniteType (X⊗Y).hom]); the earlier "[IsAlgClosed] is the only added instance" claim
369	  % is retired.
370	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
371	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "For each \(y \in V\), the complete variety \(X \times \{y\}\) gets mapped by
372	  % \(f\) into the affine variety \(U\), and hence to a single point of \(U\). But this means that for
373	  % any \(x \in X\), \(y \in V\), \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g \circ p_2(x, y)\), and this proves our
374	  % assertion." [Mumford, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma, p.~43.]
375	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43 (the
376	  ``for each \(y \in V\), the complete slice maps into the affine, hence to a single point'' step).}
377	  Let \(\bar k\) be algebraically closed and let \(X\), \(Y\), \(Z\) be objects of
378	  \(\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)\) with \(X\) proper, \(X \times Y\) geometrically irreducible, reduced,
379	  and \emph{locally of finite type} over \(\bar k\) (the formalization carries
380	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\), which makes the saturated open \(U\)
381	  below a Jacobson space, so its closed points are dense --- the hypothesis Step~2 of the proof
382	  consumes), and \(Z\) separated. Let \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) be a morphism, fix a \(\bar k\)-point
383	  \(x_0 \colon \mathbf 1 \to X\), and write $\mathtt{retract} := \mathtt{lift}\,
384	  (\mathtt{toUnit}\,(X \times Y) \fatsemi x_0)\,(p_2)\( for the ``\)(x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)$''
385	  endomorphism. Suppose:
386	  \begin{itemize}
387	    \item \(U \subseteq X \times Y\) is a \(p_2\)-\emph{saturated} open, i.e.\ \(U = p_2^{-1}(V_{\mathrm{set}})\)
388	      for a set \(V_{\mathrm{set}} \subseteq Y\) (so \(U\) contains the whole fibre \(X_y\) over each of
389	      its points);
390	    \item \(U_0 \subseteq Z\) is an \emph{affine} open;
391	    \item \(f(U) \subseteq U_0\), i.e.\ \(f\) maps \(U\) into the affine \(U_0\).
392	  \end{itemize}
393	  Then \(f\) and \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) agree on \(U\) as scheme morphisms:
394	  \(U.\iota \fatsemi f = U.\iota \fatsemi (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)\).
395	\end{lemma}
396	\begin{proof}
397	  \leanok
398	  This is the formalization realization of Mumford's step ``for each \(y \in V\), the complete
399	  variety \(X \times \{y\}\) gets mapped by \(f\) into the affine variety \(U\), and hence to a single
400	  point of \(U\)''. We realise it by a \textbf{global-sections + per-closed-point} route that is
401	  \emph{cohomology-free}; in particular the relative Stein-factorisation / proper-pushforward
402	  \(f_*\mathcal O = \mathcal O\) framing is a confirmed Mathlib gap (no Stein factorisation, no
403	  \(H^0\) base change) and is \emph{deliberately avoided}. The argument splits into two named
404	  sub-lemmas, one residual and one proven.
405	  \medskip
406	  \noindent\textbf{Step 1 (per-closed-slice constancy) --- \cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}, the residual.}
407	  Fix a closed point \(x\) of \(U\), lying over a closed point \(y = p_2(x) \in V_{\mathrm{set}}\).
408	  Since \(\bar k\) is algebraically closed and \(Y\) is of finite type over \(\bar k\), the residue field
409	  is \(\kappa(y) = \bar k\). Because \(U = p_2^{-1}(V_{\mathrm{set}})\) is saturated, \(U\) contains the
410	  \emph{entire} fibre \(X_y \cong X\) over \(y\); hence \(f\) maps the proper integral slice \(X_y\) into the
411	  affine \(U_0\). By \(\mathtt{isField\_of\_universallyClosed}\) the global sections \(\Gamma(X_y)\) form a
412	  field, module-finite over \(\bar k\) by \(\mathtt{finite\_appTop\_of\_universallyClosed}\), and
413	  algebraic closedness collapses the finite extension to give \(\Gamma(X_y) = \bar k\). A morphism into
414	  the affine \(U_0\) is pinned by its ring map on global sections (\(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isAffine}\)), so the
415	  restriction of \(f\) to \(X_y\) factors through a single \(\bar k\)-point of \(U_0\) --- necessarily the
416	  point \(f(x_0, y) = (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)(x)\). Thus \(f\) and \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\)
417	  agree (after the residue-field probe) at every closed point of \(U\). This per-slice statement is the
418	  chain's single genuinely-deep residual \texttt{sorry}.
419	  \medskip
420	  \noindent\textbf{Step 2 (globalisation over dense closed points) --- \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}, proven.}
421	  The closed points are dense in the locally-of-finite-type \(\bar k\)-scheme \(U\)
422	  (\(\mathtt{closure\_closedPoints}\) / the Jacobson-space property, supplied here by the new hypothesis
423	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\)). To turn ``\(f\) and
424	  \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) agree at each closed point'' into a single morphism equality on \(U\),
425	  the proven globalisation \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints} assembles the closed points
426	  into one dominant probe --- the coproduct
427	  \(\coprod_{x \in \mathrm{closedPoints}\,U} \Spec \kappa(x) \to U\), whose range is dense --- and feeds
428	  it to the reduced-source / separated-target rigidity handle \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}\) (the same
429	  family \(\mathtt{rigidity\_core}\) uses). This yields the morphism equality
430	  \(U.\iota \fatsemi f = U.\iota \fatsemi (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)\) on all of \(U\). The
431	  ``agrees on a dense set of closed points \(\Rightarrow\) hom-equality'' connective is the one piece
432	  Mathlib does not package directly (it supplies only the single-dominant-morphism
433	  \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}\)); that bespoke build is now done in
434	  \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}, and it is \emph{not} cohomology. \qedhere
435	\end{proof}
436	\begin{lemma}\leanok
437	[Step~2: morphisms agreeing at every closed point of a Jacobson source are equal]
438	  \label{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}
439	  
440	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}
441	  % Project-bespoke assembly: NO external source. Mathlib supplies only the single-dominant-morphism
442	  % ext_of_isDominant; the "dense closed points => hom-extensionality" connective is built here.
443	  Let \(W\), \(Z\) be schemes with \(W\) \emph{reduced}, \(W\) a \emph{Jacobson space}, and \(Z\)
444	  \emph{separated}. Let \(g_1, g_2 \colon W \to Z\) be morphisms. If, for every \emph{closed} point
445	  \(x \in W\), the two morphisms agree after restriction along the residue-field point
446	  \(\Spec \kappa(x) \to W\) (in Lean, $W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_1 =
447	  W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_2\(), then \)g_1 = g_2$.
448	\end{lemma}
449	\begin{proof}
450	\leanok
451	  This is the ``dense closed points \(\Rightarrow\) hom-extensionality'' connective that Mathlib does
452	  not package directly: it supplies only the single-dominant-morphism extensionality
453	  \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}\) (a dominant morphism into a separated target is an epimorphism for
454	  the purpose of right-cancellation). We assemble the per-closed-point hypotheses into one such
455	  dominant probe.
456	  Form the coproduct \(P := \coprod_{x \in \mathrm{closedPoints}\,W} \Spec \kappa(x)\) and the morphism
457	  \(\mathrm{probe} \colon P \to W\) defined by \(\mathtt{Sigma.desc}\) of the residue-field points
458	  \(W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x\). Its topological range contains every closed point of \(W\):
459	  the inclusion \(\iota_x \colon \Spec \kappa(x) \to P\) composes with \(\mathrm{probe}\) to
460	  \(W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x\) (the defining property of \(\mathtt{Sigma.desc}\)), and the
461	  range of \(W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x\) is the singleton \(\{x\}\)
462	  (\(\mathtt{Scheme.range\_fromSpecResidueField}\)). Because \(W\) is a Jacobson space, the closed points
463	  are dense (\(\mathtt{closure\_closedPoints}\), i.e.\ \(\overline{\mathrm{closedPoints}\,W} = W\)), so
464	  the range of \(\mathrm{probe}\) is dense and \(\mathrm{probe}\) is \emph{dominant}.
465	  Componentwise, \(g_1\) and \(g_2\) agree after \(\mathrm{probe}\): precomposing with each coproduct
466	  inclusion \(\iota_x\) reduces (by \(\mathtt{Sigma.\iota\_desc}\)) to
467	  \(W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_1 = W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_2\),
468	  which is the hypothesis at the closed point \(x\). By the universal property of the coproduct
469	  (\(\mathtt{Sigma.hom\_ext}\)) this gives \(\mathrm{probe} \fatsemi g_1 = \mathrm{probe} \fatsemi g_2\).
470	  Since \(\mathrm{probe}\) is dominant and \(Z\) is separated, \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}\) cancels it
471	  on the left to yield \(g_1 = g_2\).
472	\end{proof}
473	\begin{lemma}
474	\leanok
475	[Global-sections constancy: two \(\bar k\)-points of a proper integral source into an affine agree]
476	  \label{lem:eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}
477	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}
478	  % Project-bespoke assembly of Mathlib lemmas: NO external source. Realises, cohomology-free, the
479	  % classical "a global regular function on a proper integral variety over an algebraically closed
480	  % field is constant" --- precisely the affine-image step of Mumford's Rigidity Lemma proof quoted
481	  % above (the "complete slice into an affine maps to a single point" sentence, Ch.~II \S4, p.~43).
482	  Let \(\bar k\) be algebraically closed and let \(W\) be an integral scheme equipped with a structure
483	  morphism \(w \colon W \to \Spec \bar k\) that is universally closed (proper) and locally of finite
484	  type. Let \(g \colon W \to V\) be a morphism into an \emph{affine} scheme \(V\). Then any two
485	  \(\bar k\)-points of \(W\) --- i.e.\ any two sections \(a, b \colon \Spec \bar k \to W\) of \(w\) (so
486	  \(a \fatsemi w = \mathbf 1\) and \(b \fatsemi w = \mathbf 1\)) --- satisfy \(a \fatsemi g = b \fatsemi g\).
487	  \medskip
488	  \noindent\textbf{Every hypothesis is load-bearing} (confirmed by the iter-161 audit).
489	  \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\) collapses the finite extension \(\Gamma(W)/\bar k\); integrality of
490	  \(W\) makes \(\Gamma(W)\) a domain (without it a two-point disjoint union is a counterexample);
491	  \(\mathtt{UniversallyClosed}\) is what upgrades \(\Gamma(W)\) to a \emph{field} (without it
492	  \(\mathbb A^1\), with \(\Gamma = \bar k[t]\) and two distinct \(\bar k\)-points, is a counterexample);
493	  \(\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\) gives finiteness of \(\Gamma(W)\) over \(\bar k\); and
494	  \([\mathtt{IsAffine}\ V]\) is what lets \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isAffine}\) pin the morphism by its ring map
495	  on global sections.
496	\end{lemma}
497	\begin{proof}
498	\leanok
499	  Because \(W\) is integral and \(w\) is universally closed, the global sections
500	  \(\Gamma(W, \mathcal O_W)\) form a field (\(\mathtt{isField\_of\_universallyClosed}\)), and the structure
501	  ring map \(w^\sharp \colon \bar k \to \Gamma(W)\) (the map \(\mathtt{wk.appTop}\) after the canonical
502	  identification \(\Gamma(\Spec \bar k) \cong \bar k\)) is integral / module-finite
503	  (\(\mathtt{finite\_appTop\_of\_universallyClosed}\)). Since \(\bar k\) is algebraically closed and
504	  \(\Gamma(W)\) is a domain finite over it, that map is bijective
505	  (\(\mathtt{IsAlgClosed.ringHom\_bijective\_of\_isIntegral}\)); thus \(\Gamma(W) = \bar k\) and
506	  \(w^\sharp\) is an isomorphism on global sections --- in particular an epimorphism. Both sections
507	  \(a\), \(b\) left-invert \(w^\sharp\) on global sections (they are sections of \(w\)), so they induce the
508	  \emph{same} ring map \(a^\sharp = b^\sharp\) on \(\Gamma(W)\) (cancel the epimorphism \(w^\sharp\)). A
509	  morphism into the \emph{affine} \(V\) is pinned by its global-sections ring map
510	  (\(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isAffine}\)), whence \(a \fatsemi g = b \fatsemi g\). This realises, without
511	  cohomology, the classical ``proper connected variety into an affine is a single point'' step
512	  quoted from Mumford above.
513	\end{proof}
514	\begin{lemma}
515	\leanok
516	[Integrality descends to a retract of an integral scheme]
517	  \label{lem:isIntegral_of_retract_of_integral}
518	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isIntegral_of_retract}
519	  % NOTE: (iter-162 review) project-bespoke (elementary topology + algebra); NO external source.
520	  % LANDED axiom-clean as `AlgebraicGeometry.isIntegral_of_retract`, stated generically for any
521	  % retract `r : S ⟶ T`, `pr : T ⟶ S` with `r ≫ pr = 𝟙 S` of an integral `T` (more general than
522	  % the `X`/`X×Y`/`p_1` instance below). PROOF DIVERGENCE (cosmetic, sound either way): the Lean
523	  % proves reducedness PER-STALK (each `S.stalk x` embeds via `pr.stalkMap` into the reduced
524	  % `T.stalk`, then `isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk`), not via the global-sections split-injection
525	  % the prose below describes. Plan/blueprint-writer may align the prose to the stalk route.
526	  Let \(X \times Y\) be an integral scheme (here it is geometrically irreducible and reduced over
527	  \(\bar k\)). Suppose \(X\) is a \emph{retract} of \(X \times Y\): there is a section
528	  \(s \colon X \to X \times Y\) of the first projection \(p_1\), i.e.\ \(s \fatsemi p_1 = \mathbf 1_X\).
529	  Then \(X\) is integral.
530	\end{lemma}
531	\begin{proof}
532	\leanok
533	  Integrality is reducedness together with irreducibility, and the retract supplies each.
534	  \emph{Reduced.} The section identity \(s \fatsemi p_1 = \mathbf 1_X\) makes the pullback on global
535	  sections \(p_1^\sharp \colon \mathcal O_X(X) \to \mathcal O_{X \times Y}(X \times Y)\) split
536	  injective (it is left-inverted by \(s^\sharp\)). A split-injective ring map into the reduced ring
537	  \(\mathcal O_{X \times Y}(X \times Y)\) embeds \(\mathcal O_X(X)\) into a reduced ring, so it too is
538	  reduced (\(\mathtt{isReduced\_of\_injective}\) on the split injection); hence \(X\) is reduced.
539	  \emph{Irreducible.} The first projection \(p_1 \colon X \times Y \to X\) is surjective because it
540	  admits the section \(s\) (every point of \(X\) is \(p_1(s(\cdot))\)). The continuous image of an
541	  irreducible space under a surjection is irreducible; as \(X \times Y\) is irreducible and \(p_1\) is a
542	  continuous surjection onto \(X\), the space of \(X\) is irreducible.
543	  Reduced and irreducible together give that \(X\) is integral. This is elementary --- no cohomology,
544	  no external citation --- and feeds the Step-1 geometric assembly below, where the proper slice
545	  \(X_y \cong X\) must be presented as proper \emph{integral}.
546	\end{proof}
547	\begin{lemma}\leanok
548	[Step~1: per-closed-slice constancy of a proper slice into an affine]
549	  \label{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}
550	  
551	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}
552	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
553	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "For each \(y \in V\), the complete variety \(X \times \{y\}\) gets mapped by
554	  % \(f\) into the affine variety \(U\), and hence to a single point of \(U\). But this means that for
555	  % any \(x \in X\), \(y \in V\), \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g \circ p_2(x, y)\), and this proves our
556	  % assertion." [Mumford, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma, p.~43.]
557	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43 (the
558	  ``for each \(y \in V\), the complete slice maps into the affine, hence to a single point'' step).}
559	  With the data of \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine} --- \(\bar k\) algebraically
560	  closed, \(X\) proper over \(\bar k\), \(X \times Y\) geometrically irreducible, reduced, and locally of

563	  affine open \(U_0 \subseteq Z\) --- fix a \emph{closed} point \(x\) of \(U\). Then \(f\) and the collapsed
564	  map \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) (with $\mathtt{retract} := \mathtt{lift}\,
565	  (\mathtt{toUnit}\,(X \times Y) \fatsemi x_0)\,(p_2)\(, i.e.\ \)(x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)\() agree at \)x$
566	  after the residue-field probe \(\Spec \kappa(x) \to U\):
567	  \[ U.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi (U.\iota \fatsemi f)
568	     = U.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi (U.\iota \fatsemi (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)). \]
569	\end{lemma}

1193	  \textbf{The scaling action (primary).} The \emph{multiplicative scaling action}
1194	  \[ \sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1, \qquad
1195	     \text{``}\sigma_\times(x, \lambda) = \lambda x\text{''} \]
1196	  (\lean name \texttt{gmScalingP1}) is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of
1197	  \(\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m\) --- no indeterminacy, characteristic-free (M\"obius scaling
1198	  fixing both \(0\) and \(\infty\)) --- defined chartwise:
1199	  \begin{itemize}
1200	    \item On \(\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_m\) (target chart \(x\)): \((x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x\),
1201	      a polynomial map, regular everywhere.
1202	    \item Near \(\infty\) (target chart \(u = 1/x\)): the target coordinate \(1/(\lambda x)\) rewrites as
1203	      \(u/\lambda\), regular everywhere on this chart because \(\lambda \in \mathbb G_m\) is invertible.
1204	  \end{itemize}
1205	  The two target charts cover the image and overlap consistently, so \(\sigma_\times\) is defined on
1206	  the whole surface. It is a left action of the group \(\mathbb G_m\) on \(\mathbb P^1\):
1207	  \(\sigma_\times(x, 1) = x\) and $\sigma_\times(\sigma_\times(x, \lambda), \mu) = \sigma_\times(x,
1208	  \lambda\mu)\(. Crucially it has \emph{two fixed points}, \)\sigma_\times(0, \lambda) = 0$ and
1209	  \(\sigma_\times(\infty, \lambda) = \infty\) for all \(\lambda\); the fixed point \(0\) is what makes the
1210	  \(W\)-axis of the additive decomposition collapse in the primary base-case proof
1211	  (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, \cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}).
1212	  \medskip
1213	  \noindent\textbf{Companion: the \(\mathbb G_a\)-translation action (demoted route only).} For the
1214	  Milne-faithful additive alternative we also record the \emph{translation action}
1215	  \[ \sigma \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a \to \mathbb P^1, \qquad
1216	     \text{``}\sigma(x, y) = x + y\text{''} \]
1217	  (\lean name \texttt{gaTranslationP1} [expected]), likewise a \emph{total} scheme morphism on all
1218	  of \(\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a\), defined chartwise: on \(\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_a\)
1219	  (chart \(x\)) it is the polynomial map \((x, y) \mapsto x + y\); near \(\infty\) (\(u = 1/x\)) the
1220	  coordinate \(1/(x + y)\) rewrites as \(u/(1 + u y)\), regular at \(u = 0\) (value \(0\) there --- \(\sigma\)
1221	  fixes \(\infty\) for every \(y\)), while the locus \(x + y = 0\) is covered by the chart \(x\) on which it
1222	  is the finite value \(0\). It is a left action of \(\mathbb G_a\) on \(\mathbb P^1\) fixing \(\infty\):
1223	  \(\sigma(x, 0) = x\) and \(\sigma(\sigma(x, y), y') = \sigma(x, y + y')\). This companion underlies
1224	  only the alternative proof of \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial} and the demoted $\mathbb
1225	  G_a$-additive close of the base case (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}); it is \emph{not} on the
1226	  genus-\(0\) critical path.

1360	  \(\mathrm{pullback}\,((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_x.f)\,((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_y.f)\) agree:
1361	  \[ \mathrm{pullback.fst}\,((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_x.f)\,((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_y.f)
1362	     \fatsemi \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,\bar k\,x
1363	     \ =\
1364	     \mathrm{pullback.snd}\,((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_x.f)\,((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_y.f)
1365	     \fatsemi \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,\bar k\,y. \]
1366	  This is the cocycle / overlap-coherence equation that \(\mathtt{Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms}\)
1367	  consumes in order to assemble the chart-wise pieces \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,\bar k\,i\) into
1368	  a single morphism on the total \((\mathbb P^1 \otimes \mathbb G_m).\mathrm{left}\). The diagonal
1369	  cases \((x, y) = (0, 0)\) and \((x, y) = (1, 1)\) are immediate via
1370	  \(\mathtt{pullback.condition}\); the substantive content is the cross case \((0, 1)\) (and its
1371	  symmetric \((1, 0)\)), broken down below.
1372	  \medskip

1384	  % (`refine ≪≫ ?_; refine; exact`), so its elaboration does not display its
1385	  % `Iso.trans`-spine syntactically; the canonical Mathlib simp chain

1389	  \noindent\textbf{(I) Setup and structural lift.} Restricting the indices to the cross case
1390	  \((x, y) = (0, 1)\), the two charts being compared are
1391	  \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,\bar k\,0\) over the cover patch
1392	  \((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover}\,\bar k).X\,0 = D_+(X_0) \times \mathbb G_m\) (containing
1393	  \(0 \in \mathbb A^1\)) and \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,\bar k\,1\) over
1394	  \((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover}\,\bar k).X\,1 = D_+(X_1) \times \mathbb G_m\) (containing
1395	  \(\infty\)). Their pairwise pullback intersection sits over the basic-open intersection
1396	  \(D_+(X_0 \cdot X_1) \times \mathbb G_m\), and the project records an explicit iso
1397	  identifying the intersection scheme with an affine \(\Spec\):
1398	  \[ \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover\_intersection\_X\_iso}\,\bar k \colon
1399	     \mathrm{pullback}\,((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover}\,\bar k)_0.f)\,
1400	       ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover}\,\bar k)_1.f)
1401	     \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
1402	     \Spec\bigl(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,(X_0 \cdot X_1) \otimes_{\bar k}
1403	       \mathtt{GmRing}\,\bar k\bigr). \]
1404	  The cleanest entry point is to post-compose both sides of the cocycle with the iso's
1405	  inverse and cancel the resulting epimorphism --- the
1406	  \(\mathtt{cancel\_epi}\,(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover\_intersection\_X\_iso}\,\bar k).\mathrm{inv}\)
1407	  move. This lifts the agreement to a morphism equality between two arrows
1408	  \(\Spec\bigl(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,(X_0 \cdot X_1) \otimes_{\bar k}
1409	     \mathtt{GmRing}\,\bar k\bigr) \rightrightarrows \overline{\mathbb P^1}\), where both
1410	  sides should factor through the merged-generator chart
1411	  \(\mathrm{Proj.awayι}\,(X_0 \cdot X_1)\). The cocycle then reduces to an equality of two
1412	  ring maps into \(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,(X_0 \cdot X_1) \otimes_{\bar k}
1413	  \mathtt{GmRing}\,\bar k\).
1414	  \medskip
1415	  \noindent\textbf{(II) Why the canonical simp chain is blocked.} After
1416	  \(\mathtt{cancel\_epi}\) the goal contains the inverse of
1417	  \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover\_intersection\_X\_iso}\) composed with each pullback
1418	  projection. In principle the Mathlib-canonical simp set
1419	  \(\{\mathtt{Iso.trans\_inv},\, \mathtt{Iso.inv\_comp\_eq},\,
1420	  \mathtt{pullback.congrHom\_inv},\, \mathtt{asIso\_inv},\,
1421	  \mathtt{pullbackRightPullbackFstIso\_inv\_*},\,
1422	  \mathtt{pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso\_inv\_*},\,
1423	  \mathtt{pullbackSymmetry\_inv\_comp\_*},\,
1424	  \mathtt{Proj.pullbackAwayιIso\_inv\_*}\}\)
1425	  together with the project-side simp helpers
1426	  \(\mathtt{pullback\_map\_fst\_proj}\) / \(\mathtt{pullback\_map\_snd\_proj}\)
1427	  (\cref{lem:pullback_map_fst_proj}, \cref{lem:pullback_map_snd_proj}; iter-184 Recipe~1)
1428	  would collapse the iso projections to a single \(\Spec.\mathrm{map}\) of a known ring
1429	  map. Empirically, however, \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover\_intersection\_X\_iso}\) is
1430	  produced by a tactic-mode \(\mathtt{refine\ \ldots\ \fatsemi\ \ldots\ \fatsemi\ ?\_}\)
1431	  construction whose elaboration does not present its
1432	  \(\mathtt{Iso.trans}\)-spine syntactically; consequently \emph{none} of the canonical
1433	  inv-projection simp lemmas matches the elaborated form, and the iter-181 through
1434	  iter-185 attempts at closure via \(\mathtt{simp [Iso.trans\_inv, \ldots]}\) report every
1435	  simp argument ``unused''. This is the precise diagnostic recorded in
1436	  \(\mathtt{analogies/gmscaling{\text-}projection{\text-}idiom.md}\) (iter-184 analogist
1437	  consult, which supersedes the now-stale \(\mathtt{analogies/chart{\text-}bridge.md}\)
1438	  cited in earlier iterations of this proof sketch).
1439	  \medskip
1440	  \noindent\textbf{(III) Three concrete pickup paths.} Three orthogonal strategies are
1441	  open for closing this lemma; the plan agent selects one per iteration based on
1442	  helper-budget and risk.
1443	  \begin{description}
1444	    \item[(III.a) Refactor the iso to term-mode \(\fatsemi\)-spine.] Rewrite
1445	      \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover\_intersection\_X\_iso}\) as a single
1446	      \(\mathtt{Iso.trans}\) (\(\fatsemi\)-)chain of named Mathlib isos
1447	      (\(\mathtt{pullbackRightPullbackFstIso}\),
1448	      \(\mathtt{pullback.congrHom}\),
1449	      \(\mathtt{pullbackSymmetry}\),
1450	      \(\mathtt{Proj.pullbackAwayιIso}\), and the final
1451	      \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso}\)), with \emph{no} intermediate
1452	      \(\mathtt{refine \ldots ?\_}\) holes discharged in tactic mode. With the spine
1453	      syntactically present, \(\mathtt{Iso.trans\_inv}\) unfolds the chain link-by-link
1454	      and the canonical inv-projection simp lemmas fire; the cocycle then closes via the
1455	      analogist's Recipe~1/2/3 (cf.\ \(\mathtt{analogies/gmscaling{\text-}projection
1456	      {\text-}idiom.md}\)). Estimated cost: \(\sim 30\)--\(50\) LOC; main risk is that
1457	      the term-mode form requires explicit type annotations at each link, which a
1458	      structural-refactor pass should be able to supply.
1459	    \item[(III.b) Package named projection lemmas via \(\mathtt{pullback.hom\_ext}\).]
1460	      Without refactoring the iso, prove two \(\mathtt{@[reassoc (attr := simp)]}\)
1461	      lemmas
1462	      \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover\_intersection\_X\_iso\_inv\_fst}\) /
1463	      \(\mathtt{\_inv\_snd}\) whose body is a hand-written
1464	      \(\mathtt{pullback.hom\_ext}\) against the relevant pullback projection of the
1465	      intersection. The universal property of the pullback forces each projection's
1466	      value diagram-wise; the proof sidesteps the opaque elaborated iso form because
1467	      \(\mathtt{pullback.hom\_ext}\) does not require syntactic unfolding of the iso's
1468	      definition. Estimated cost: \(\sim 50\)--\(80\) LOC for the two named lemmas plus
1469	      \(\sim 10\)--\(20\) LOC of reuse in the cocycle closure
1470	      \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart\_agreement\_cross01}\). This is the analogist-
1471	      recommended path of
1472	      \(\mathtt{analogies/gmscaling{\text-}projection{\text-}idiom.md}\) Recipe~2 but
1473	      requires relaxing the iter-184 / iter-185 helper-budget = 0 cap (two new named
1474	      top-level lemmas).
1475	    \item[(III.c) Bypass via a separating sheaf argument.] The chart agreement is
1476	      equivalent to ``two morphisms \(X \to \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) agree iff their
1477	      composites with a separating family of opens agree''. The genus-\(0\) chart cover
1478	      supplies an obvious separating family (the two \(\mathbb G_m\)-affine pieces), so
1479	      a separating-sheaf criterion bypasses the pullback-iso chase entirely. Estimated
1480	      cost: \(\sim 80\)--\(120\) LOC; main risk is dependency on
1481	      \(\mathtt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsSeparatedOver}\) / \(\mathtt{Scheme.sheafExt}\)
1482	      lemmas whose availability at the project's pinned Mathlib commit must be verified
1483	      by the plan agent before commitment. This pickup path falls under the
1484	      ``Genus-\(0\) separated-locus alternative'' open strategic question in
1485	      \(\mathtt{STRATEGY.md}\).
1486	  \end{description}

1488	  \noindent\textbf{(IV) Substantive ring-level residual.} All three pickup paths reduce,
1489	  after their structural setup, to the same ring-level identity. On the basic-open
1490	  intersection \(D_+(X_0 \cdot X_1) \subseteq \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) both chart
1491	  coordinates are units (the chart-\(0\) coordinate \(t = X_1/X_0\) and the chart-\(1\)
1492	  coordinate \(u = X_0/X_1\) are reciprocals: \(t \cdot u = 1\)), and the agreement
1493	  reduces to the ring-level identity
1494	  \[ \lambda \cdot u \ =\ \frac{1}{t} \cdot \lambda \quad
1495	     \text{in } \mathrm{Localization.Away}\,t \otimes_{\bar k} \mathtt{GmRing}\,\bar k, \]
1496	  i.e.\ to the unit-of-the-localisation identity \(t \cdot u = 1\)
1497	  (\(\mathtt{IsLocalization.Away.mul\_invSelf}\)) together with commutativity of the
1498	  tensor product. The proof routes through the chart-ring iso
1499	  \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso} and the same \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso}\) bridge that
1500	  \cref{def:gmscaling_chart} uses; consequently \cref{lem:gmscaling_chart_agreement}
1501	  remains gated on \cref{def:gmscaling_chart} acquiring its concrete body \emph{and} on
1502	  one of the (III.a)--(III.c) structural setups landing. See
1503	  \(\mathtt{analogies/gmscaling{\text-}projection{\text-}idiom.md}\) (iter-184) for the
1504	  full Recipe~1/2/3 decomposition and empirical simp-probe verification.
1505	\end{lemma}

1628	  Let \(\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1\) be the scaling action
1629	  of \cref{def:gaTranslationP1}. Then the composite
1630	  \[ \mathbb G_m \xrightarrow{\;(\mathrm{toUnit}_{\mathbb G_m} \fatsemi 0_{\mathbb P^1},\ \mathbf 1_{\mathbb G_m})\;}

1633	  \(\sigma_\times(0, \lambda) = 0\) for every \(\lambda \in \mathbb G_m\).

1635	\begin{proof}
1636	  Chart-level. On the affine chart \(\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_m\) (coordinate \(x\)), the
1637	  morphism \(\sigma_\times\) is the polynomial map \((x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda \cdot x\). Restricting
1638	  to the \(\mathbb G_m\)-section \(x = 0\) gives the constant ring map
1639	  \(\bar k[t, t^{-1}] \to \bar k\), \(\lambda \mapsto 0\); equivalently the scheme morphism factors
1640	  
1641	  through the closed point \(0 \in \mathbb P^1\). The agreement with the other chart \(u = 1/x\) near
1642	  
1643	  \(\infty\) is automatic (the section \(x = 0\) does not meet that chart). The named equation is the
1644	  Lean encoding of this fact, with \(\mathrm{toUnit}_{\mathbb G_m}\) supplying the
1645	  \(\Spec \bar k\)-factor and \(\mathbf 1_{\mathbb G_m}\) the parameter \(\lambda\).
1646	\end{proof}

1654	\begin{lemma}

1661	  The structure morphism of the product

1	# Analogy: intersection-ring identification for the `gmScalingP1_cover` cross01 cocycle and `iotaGm_range_isOpen`
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	intersection-ring-cross01
8	
9	## Iteration
10	182
11	
12	## Question
13	
14	Joint consult covering two routes blocked on the SAME upstream gap: the
15	`Away 𝒜 (X 0 * X 1)` intersection-ring identification for
16	`ProjectiveLineBar kbar = Proj ⟨k̄[X 0, X 1]⟩`.
17	
18	1. **Route 1** — `GmScaling.lean` `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01` (L295).
19	2. **Route 3** — `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean` `iotaGm_range_isOpen` (L98).
20	
21	## Project artifact(s)
22	
23	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean:295-308` — cross01 cocycle
24	  helper.
25	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean:106-110` — `gmScalingP1_cover`
26	  via `pullback₁`.
27	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean:124-141` — `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`
28	  (the per-chart iso to `Spec (Away 𝒜 (X i) ⊗ GmRing)`).
29	- `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:98-106` — `iotaGm_range_isOpen`.
30	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean:86-134` — `pointOfVec` and
31	  `zeroPt`/`onePt`/`inftyPt` (via `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`).
32	
33	## Decisions identified
34	
35	### Decision 1: `HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalization_mul` is in Mathlib and is the right idiom
36	
37	- **Mathlib idiom**: `HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalization_mul`
38	  (`.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/GradedAlgebra/HomogeneousLocalization.lean:883`)
39	  packages: for `f ∈ 𝒜 d` (d ≠ 0) and `g ∈ 𝒜 e` and `x = f * g`,
40	  `Away 𝒜 x` is the localization of `Away 𝒜 f` at the image
41	  `Away.isLocalizationElem hf hg = g^d / f^e ∈ Away 𝒜 f` (L877).
42	- **Project's current path**: the iter-181 Lane B task_result correctly
43	  identified this lemma (lines 67–68 of the report) but treated it as a
44	  stand-alone Mathlib bridge to be threaded by hand.
45	- **Gap**: identical (the algebraic content is shipped).
46	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** with `Away.isLocalization_mul`. No project
47	  helper is needed at the ring level — Decision 2 supersedes the ring
48	  step entirely.
49	
50	### Decision 2 (the load-bearing one): Mathlib ALREADY ships the Spec-level intersection iso `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso`
51	
52	- **Mathlib idiom**: `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.pullbackAwayιIso`
53	  (`.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:258-265`)
54	  is verbatim:
55	
56	  ```lean
57	  noncomputable
58	  def pullbackAwayιIso :
59	      Limits.pullback (awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm) (awayι 𝒜 g g_deg hm') ≅
60	        Spec (.of <| Away 𝒜 x) :=  -- where hx : x = f * g
61	    IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq ...
62	  ```
63	
64	  Companion `reassoc (attr := simp)` lemmas (L268-302):
65	  - `pullbackAwayιIso_hom_awayι` (L268): `iso.hom ≫ awayι 𝒜 x _ _ =
66	    pullback.fst _ _ ≫ awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm`.
67	  - `pullbackAwayιIso_hom_SpecMap_awayMap_left` (L275): `iso.hom ≫
68	    Spec.map (ofHom (awayMap 𝒜 g_deg hx)) = pullback.fst _ _`.
69	  - `pullbackAwayιIso_hom_SpecMap_awayMap_right` (L282): `iso.hom ≫
70	    Spec.map (ofHom (awayMap 𝒜 f_deg ...)) = pullback.snd _ _`.
71	  - `pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst` / `pullbackAwayιIso_inv_snd` (L291-301):
72	    the inverse projections in terms of `Spec.map (awayMap ...)`.
73	
74	  Hover-verified end-to-end at iter-182 via `lean_run_code` (signatures
75	  reproduce identically; instances all resolve).
76	
77	- **Project's current path**: iter-181 Lane B task_result claimed
78	  "the bridge `pullback ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≅ Spec (...)` is not
79	  packaged in Mathlib as a single lemma" (report line 64). This is
80	  **incorrect at the inner pullback level** — Mathlib ships the iso
81	  `pullback (awayι X_0) (awayι X_1) ≅ Spec (Away 𝒜 (X 0 * X 1))` plus
82	  three `simp` lemmas that pin every projection. The project's missing
83	  step is the **pasting** that pushes this inner iso through the
84	  pullback over `(ℙ¹ ⊗ Gm).left = pullback PLB.hom Gm.hom`.
85	
86	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost (one iter of investigative dead-end
87	  attempts on `cancel_mono` / `unfold + simp` because the project
88	  treated the Mathlib gap as deeper than it is).
89	
90	- **Cost of divergence (if any)**: the iter-181 task_result's "iter-182+
91	  estimate ~70-115 LOC" included ~25-40 LOC for "build the intersection
92	  ring iso explicitly" — that line is wasted work. Mathlib already
93	  ships the Spec-level iso AND the ring-level iso (via `awayMap`).
94	
95	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — discard the "build a project-side
96	  `Away_X0_X1_iso : Away 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1) ≃+* Localization.Away (chart-1 affine coord)`"
97	  plan from the iter-181 task_result step 1. Use `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso`
98	  + companion `simp` lemmas directly. No new project helper is needed
99	  at the inner ring level.

lemma pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst :
    (pullbackAwayιIso 𝒜 f_deg hm g_deg hm' hx).inv ≫ Limits.pullback.fst _ _ =
      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (awayMap 𝒜 g_deg hx)) := by
  rw [← pullbackAwayιIso_hom_SpecMap_awayMap_left, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma pullbackAwayιIso_inv_snd :
    (pullbackAwayιIso 𝒜 f_deg hm g_deg hm' hx).inv ≫ Limits.pullback.snd _ _ =
      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (awayMap 𝒜 f_deg (hx.trans (mul_comm _ _)))) := by
  rw [← pullbackAwayιIso_hom_SpecMap_awayMap_right (hx := hx) .., Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]

include hm' in
lemma awayι_preimage_basicOpen :
--
  · rw [← pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst 𝒜 f_deg hm g_deg hm' rfl]

      TopCat.hom_comp, ContinuousMap.coe_comp, Set.range_comp]
    rw [Set.range_eq_univ.mpr (by exact
      (pullbackAwayιIso 𝒜 f_deg hm g_deg hm' rfl).inv.homeomorph.surjective),
      ← opensRange_awayι _ _ g_deg hm']
    simp [IsOpenImmersion.range_pullbackFst]

lemma SpecMap_awayMap_awayι :
    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (awayMap 𝒜 g_deg hx)) ≫ awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm =
      awayι 𝒜 x (hx ▸ SetLike.mul_mem_graded f_deg g_deg) (hm.trans_le (m.le_add_right m')) := by
  rw [awayι, awayι, Iso.eq_inv_comp, basicOpenIsoSpec_hom, basicOpenToSpec_SpecMap_awayMap_assoc,
  ← basicOpenIsoSpec_hom _ _ f_deg hm, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_ι]

--
    Category.assoc, SpecMap_awayMap_awayι]

lemma pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst :
    (pullbackAwayιIso 𝒜 f_deg hm g_deg hm' hx).inv ≫ Limits.pullback.fst _ _ =

    hs rfl, basicOpenIsoSpecAway_inv_homOfLE_assoc (R := Γ(X, ⊤)) x (f s) x' (by simp [← H', H]),

  simp only [← Category.assoc, ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
  congr 3
  ext

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
instance pullback.map_isIso {W X Y Z S T : C} (f₁ : W ⟶ S) (f₂ : X ⟶ S) [HasPullback f₁ f₂]
    (g₁ : Y ⟶ T) (g₂ : Z ⟶ T) [HasPullback g₁ g₂] (i₁ : W ⟶ Y) (i₂ : X ⟶ Z) (i₃ : S ⟶ T)
    (eq₁ : f₁ ≫ i₃ = i₁ ≫ g₁) (eq₂ : f₂ ≫ i₃ = i₂ ≫ g₂) [IsIso i₁] [IsIso i₂] [IsIso i₃] :
    IsIso (pullback.map f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ i₁ i₂ i₃ eq₁ eq₂) := by
  refine ⟨⟨pullback.map _ _ _ _ (inv i₁) (inv i₂) (inv i₃) ?_ ?_, ?_, ?_⟩⟩
  · rw [IsIso.comp_inv_eq, Category.assoc, eq₁, IsIso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
  · rw [IsIso.comp_inv_eq, Category.assoc, eq₂, IsIso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
  · cat_disch
  · cat_disch

/-- If `f₁ = f₂` and `g₁ = g₂`, we may construct a canonical
isomorphism `pullback f₁ g₁ ≅ pullback f₂ g₂` -/
@[simps! hom]
def pullback.congrHom {X Y Z : C} {f₁ f₂ : X ⟶ Z} {g₁ g₂ : Y ⟶ Z} (h₁ : f₁ = f₂) (h₂ : g₁ = g₂)
    [HasPullback f₁ g₁] [HasPullback f₂ g₂] : pullback f₁ g₁ ≅ pullback f₂ g₂ :=
  asIso <| pullback.map _ _ _ _ (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) (by simp [h₁]) (by simp [h₂])

* `leftSquareIsPullback` shows that the left square is a pullback if the other two are.
* `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso` shows, using the `pullback` API, that
  `W ×[X] (X ×[Z] Y) ≅ W ×[Z] Y`.
* `pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso` shows, using the `pullback` API, that
  `(X ×[Z] Y) ×[Y] W ≅ X ×[Z] W`.

-/

--
/-- The canonical isomorphism `W ×[X] (X ×[Z] Y) ≅ W ×[Z] Y` -/
noncomputable def pullbackRightPullbackFstIso :
    pullback f' (pullback.fst f g) ≅ pullback (f' ≫ f) g :=
  IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso
    (pasteHorizIsPullback rfl (pullback.isLimit f g) (pullback.isLimit f' (pullback.fst f g)))
    (pullback.isLimit (f' ≫ f) g)

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
theorem pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_hom_fst :
    (pullbackRightPullbackFstIso f g f').hom ≫ pullback.fst (f' ≫ f) g =
      pullback.fst f' (pullback.fst f g) :=
  IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso_hom_comp _ _ WalkingCospan.left

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
theorem pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_hom_snd :
    (pullbackRightPullbackFstIso f g f').hom ≫ pullback.snd _ _ =
      pullback.snd f' (pullback.fst f g) ≫ pullback.snd f g :=
  IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso_hom_comp _ _ WalkingCospan.right

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
theorem pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_inv_fst :
    (pullbackRightPullbackFstIso f g f').inv ≫ pullback.fst f' (pullback.fst f g) =
      pullback.fst (f' ≫ f) g :=
  IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso_inv_comp _ _ WalkingCospan.left

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
theorem pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_inv_snd_snd :
    (pullbackRightPullbackFstIso f g f').inv ≫ pullback.snd _ _ ≫ pullback.snd _ _ =
      pullback.snd _ _ :=
  IsLimit.conePointUniqueUpToIso_inv_comp _ _ WalkingCospan.right

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
theorem pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_inv_snd_fst :
    (pullbackRightPullbackFstIso f g f').inv ≫ pullback.snd _ _ ≫ pullback.fst _ _ =
      pullback.fst _ _ ≫ f' := by
  rw [← pullback.condition]
  exact pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_inv_fst_assoc f g f' _

end

603	/-- **The load-bearing fixed-point property of `σ_×`:** at the scaling fixed point
604	`0 ∈ ℙ¹`, the morphism `σ_×(0, ·) : 𝔾_m → ℙ¹` is the constant morphism at `0`. That is,
605	the composite `(0 ≫ toUnit) × 𝟙 : 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹` equals `toUnit ≫ 0`.
606

608	`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes when applied with `V = ℙ¹` proper,
609	`W = 𝔾_m`, base points `0 ∈ ℙ¹`, `1 ∈ 𝔾_m`.

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma pullbackSpecIso_inv_fst :
    (pullbackSpecIso R S T).inv ≫ pullback.fst _ _ = Spec.map (ofHom includeLeftRingHom) :=
  limit.isoLimitCone_inv_π _ _

@[reassoc]
lemma pullbackSpecIso_inv_fst' :
    (pullbackSpecIso R S T).inv ≫ pullback.fst _ _ = Spec.map (ofHom (algebraMap S _)) :=
  pullbackSpecIso_inv_fst ..

/--
The composition of the inverse of the isomorphism `pullbackSpecIso R S T` (from the pullback of
`Spec S ⟶ Spec R` and `Spec T ⟶ Spec R` to `Spec (S ⊗[R] T)`) with the second projection is
the morphism `Spec (S ⊗[R] T) ⟶ Spec T` obtained by applying `Spec.map` to the ring morphism
`t ↦ 1 ⊗ₜ[R] t`.
-/
@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma pullbackSpecIso_inv_snd :
    (pullbackSpecIso R S T).inv ≫ pullback.snd _ _ =
      Spec.map (ofHom (R := T) (S := S ⊗[R] T) (toRingHom includeRight)) :=
  limit.isoLimitCone_inv_π _ _

/--
The composition of the isomorphism `pullbackSpecIso R S T` (from the pullback of
`Spec S ⟶ Spec R` and `Spec T ⟶ Spec R` to `Spec (S ⊗[R] T)`) with the morphism
`Spec (S ⊗[R] T) ⟶ Spec S` obtained by applying `Spec.map` to the ring morphism `s ↦ s ⊗ₜ[R] 1`
is the first projection.
-/
--
lemma pullbackSpecIso_hom_fst :
    (pullbackSpecIso R S T).hom ≫ Spec.map (ofHom includeLeftRingHom) = pullback.fst _ _ := by
  rw [← pullbackSpecIso_inv_fst, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma pullbackSpecIso_hom_fst' :
    (pullbackSpecIso R S T).hom ≫ Spec.map (ofHom (algebraMap S _)) = pullback.fst _ _ :=
  pullbackSpecIso_hom_fst ..

/--
The composition of the isomorphism `pullbackSpecIso R S T` (from the pullback of
`Spec S ⟶ Spec R` and `Spec T ⟶ Spec R` to `Spec (S ⊗[R] T)`) with the morphism
`Spec (S ⊗[R] T) ⟶ Spec T` obtained by applying `Spec.map` to the ring morphism `t ↦ 1 ⊗ₜ[R] t`
--
lemma pullbackSpecIso_hom_snd :
    (pullbackSpecIso R S T).hom ≫ Spec.map (ofHom (toRingHom includeRight)) = pullback.snd _ _ := by

## Main results
- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIrreducible`:
  We say that morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically irreducible if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K`
  a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is irreducible.
  We also provide the fact that this is stable under base change (by infer_instance)
- `GeometricallyIrreducible.iff_geometricallyIrreducible_fiber`:
  A scheme is geometrically irreducible over `S` iff the fibers of all
  `s : S` are geometrically irreducible.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace`:
  If `X` is geometrically irreducible and universally open (e.g. when flat + finite presentation),
  over an irreducible scheme, then `X` is also irreducible.
  In particular, the base change of a geometrically irreducible and universally open scheme to an
  irreducible scheme is irreducible (by infer_instance).
-/

universe u

@[expose] public section
--
variable {X Y Z S : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ S) (g : Y ⟶ S)

/-- We say that morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically irreducible if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K`
a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is irreducible. -/
@[mk_iff]
class GeometricallyIrreducible (f : X ⟶ Y) : Prop where
  geometrically_irreducibleSpace : geometrically (IrreducibleSpace ·) f

lemma GeometricallyIrreducible.eq_geometrically :
    @GeometricallyIrreducible = geometrically (IrreducibleSpace ·) := by
  ext; exact geometricallyIrreducible_iff _

instance : IsStableUnderBaseChange @GeometricallyIrreducible :=
  GeometricallyIrreducible.eq_geometrically ▸ inferInstance

instance [GeometricallyIrreducible g] : GeometricallyIrreducible (pullback.fst f g) :=
  MorphismProperty.pullback_fst f g inferInstance

instance [GeometricallyIrreducible f] : GeometricallyIrreducible (pullback.snd f g) :=
  MorphismProperty.pullback_snd f g inferInstance
--
  exact (IrreducibleSpace.isIrreducible_univ _).image _ (f.fiberι _).continuous.continuousOn

/-- If `f : X ⟶ S` is geometrically irreducible and open,
then `f` induces an equivalence between the irreducible components of `X` and `S`. -/
@[simps!]
def Scheme.Hom.irreducibleComponentsEquiv [GeometricallyIrreducible f] (hf : IsOpenMap f) :
    irreducibleComponents X ≃ irreducibleComponents S :=
  (irreducibleComponentsEquivOfIsPreirreducibleFiber f f.continuous hf
    (fun _ ↦ (f.isIrreducible_preimage hf isIrreducible_singleton).isPreirreducible)

***
**The substantive `(0, 1)` cross case of `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement`** (iter-181
Lane B helper).

This is the *single* non-trivial ring-level identity for the cocycle:

`pullback.fst ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≫ chart 0 =
   pullback.snd ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≫ chart 1`.

On the basic-open intersection `D₊(X 0 · X 1) ⊆ ProjectiveLineBarScheme` both chart
coordinates are units (chart-0 coord `t = X 1 / X 0`, chart-1 coord `u = X 0 / X 1`,
with `t · u = 1`). Under `σ_×([X 0 : X 1], λ) = [λ · X 0 : X 1]` the two chart maps
satisfy on the intersection:

* chart-0: `t ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹` (i.e. `t/λ`)
* chart-1: `u ↦ u ⊗ λ` (i.e. `λ · u`)

Substituting `u = 1/t` on the chart-1 side gives `λ · u = λ · (1/t) = λ/t`, and on the
chart-0 side `t/λ` corresponds to `u' = 1/(t/λ) = λ/t` under the `u ↔ 1/t` change of
coords. Hence the ring-level identity is `λ · u = (1/t) · λ` in
`Localization.Away t ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar`, which after multiplying both sides by `t`
reduces to `λ · u · t = λ`, true because `u · t = 1`.

**Status (iter-182 Lane B):** **structural advance** — the intersection iso
`gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` is now built axiom-clean using
Mathlib's `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso` (per `analogies/intersection-ring-cross01.md`
Decision 2). Both sides of the cocycle factor through `awayι_(X 0 * X 1)` once
we cancel-epi the iso's inverse; the residual is the ring-level identity above.

The `cancel_epi` step lifts the goal from `pullback ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ⟶ Proj 𝒜`
to `Spec ((Away X_0X_1) ⊗ GmRing) ⟶ Proj 𝒜`. The fully-unfolded form (after
`simp only [gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso, Iso.trans_inv, ...]`) is a chain
of 7 iso `.inv` compositions; reducing it to a `Spec.map` of a single ring map
into `awayι_(X 0 * X 1)` requires either (a) `@[simps]` annotation on the iso, or
(b) explicit projection lemmas for each stage. Both are out of iter-182 helper
budget (helper budget = 2 used on the intersection iso + this lemma).

/-- A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically `P` if for any field `K` and
morphism `Spec K ⟶ Y`, the base change `X ×[Y] Spec K` satisfies `P`. -/
def geometrically (P : ObjectProperty Scheme.{u}) : MorphismProperty Scheme.{u} :=
  fun X Y f ↦ ∀ ⦃K : Type u⦄ [Field K] (y : Spec (.of K) ⟶ Y)
    ⦃Z : Scheme.{u}⦄ (fst : Z ⟶ X) (snd : Z ⟶ Spec (.of K)),
    IsPullback fst snd f y → P Z

lemma geometrically_eq_universally (P : ObjectProperty Scheme.{u}) :
    geometrically P = .universally fun X Y _ ↦ IsIntegral Y → Subsingleton Y → P X := by
  ext X Y f
  refine ⟨fun hf Z W snd q fst h _ _ ↦ ?_, fun hf aK y Z W fst snd h ↦ ?_⟩
  · let := (isField_of_isIntegral_of_subsingleton W).toField
    apply hf (W.isoSpec.inv ≫ q) snd (fst ≫ W.isoSpec.hom)

# AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean

## Iteration 186 — Lane B Recipe 2/3 attempt

**Verdict**: PARTIAL — substantive empirical advance with detailed
iter-187+ pickup recipe; **sorry count: 4 → 4 (no decrement; gate MISSED)**.

**Helper budget**: 0 / +2 consumed (helpers not added; see §"Why no
helpers landed" below).

**Axioms introduced this iter**: 0 (zero-axiom build retained).

## gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01 (L412) — RECIPE 2/3 BLOCKED

### Attempt 1 — Recipe 2 simp set verification

- **Approach**: per the analogist note `analogies/gmscaling-projection-idiom.md`
  Recipes 1-3 + the iter-186 chapter expansion paragraph (II/III.a/b/c):
  attempt the simp set with ALL relevant Mathlib `_inv_*` projection lemmas:
  `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_inv_fst`, `_hom_fst`, `pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso_inv_fst`,
  `pullbackSymmetry_inv_comp_fst`, `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst`,
  `pullback_map_fst_proj` (iter-184 Recipe 1 helper), `pullback.lift_fst`,
  `Spec.map_comp`, `Category.comp_id`, `Category.id_comp`.
- **Result**: FAILED — `lean_multi_attempt` empirically verified every single
  simp argument as "unused" by the linter. None fires through the
  tactic-elaborated iso form.
- **Empirical confirmation of chapter (II)**: the iter-186 chapter expansion
  prose's "the canonical simp set fails" claim is EMPIRICALLY VERIFIED iter-186.

### Attempt 2 — Goal-state inspection after existing `simp` pass at L475

- **Approach**: read the goal state after the existing
  `simp only [gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso, Iso.trans_inv, Iso.symm_inv,
  Category.assoc, Iso.inv_comp_eq, pullback.congrHom_inv, asIso_inv]` pass
  and verify what structure remains.
- **Finding**: The residual goal has visible structure:
  - **LHS** starts with `pullback.map (Spec.map (algMap kbar Away_X0X1))
    (Spec.map (algMap kbar GmRing)) (awayι (X 0 · X 1) ≫ PLB.hom) Gm.hom 𝟙 𝟙 𝟙
    _ _ ≫` (this is `pullback.congrHom_inv` applied to the LAST iso step),
    followed by an 8-link `.inv`-chain ending in `pullback.fst ((cover).f 0)
    ((cover).f 1) ≫ chart 0`.
  - **RHS** has `(pullbackSpecIso).hom ≫ (pullbackSpecIso).inv ≫ pullback.map
    (parallel form) ≫ [parallel 8-link chain] ≫ pullback.snd ≫ chart 1`.
  - The `(pullbackSpecIso).hom ≫ (pullbackSpecIso).inv` on the RHS does NOT
    simplify because the cancelling pair is BURIED in the chain's middle —
    Mathlib's `Iso.hom_inv_id` simp lemma matches on outer `≫` composition,
    and `Category.assoc` rotations don't expose it.
  - The `pullback.map ... ≫ pullbackRightPullbackFstIso.inv` adjacency within
    the inner chain is NOT served by any Mathlib simp lemma:
    `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_inv_fst/_snd_fst/_snd_snd` all require the
    RHS-projection to be SYNTACTICALLY DISPLAYED, but here it is BURIED
    inside an outer `pullback.map`. Pattern-matching consequently fails.
- **Result**: PARTIAL — goal-shape confirmed; the simp residual is genuinely
  not Mathlib-canonical.

### Attempt 3 — Chart-bridge bypass via `PLB.hom` composition

- **Approach**: try `cancel_mono (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom` to reduce the
  cocycle to its composition with `PLB.hom`, which is the form
  `gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq` directly closes (combined with
  `pullback.condition`).
- **Result**: FAILED — `Mono (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom` instance does not
  synthesize (PLB.hom is the structure morphism `Proj 𝒜 ⟶ Spec k̄`, which
  is decidedly NOT a monomorphism — its base is a single point).
- **Dead end documented**.

### Attempt 4 — `congrArg` on `_hpb` (pullback.condition)

- **Approach**: build a hypothesis `(fst ≫ chart 0) ≫ PLB.hom = (snd ≫ chart 1)
  ≫ PLB.hom` by `congrArg` on `pullback.condition` to expose the per-chart
  bridge. Apply simp/Category.assoc to align.
- **Result**: FAILED — `congrArg` type mismatch because `_hpb` is the wrong
  shape (it gives `fst ≫ (cover).f 0 = snd ≫ (cover).f 1`, not the cocycle).
- **Dead end documented**.

### Attempt 5 — Inline `change` substitution

- **Approach**: try `change` substitutions to convert the cocycle into a form
  where `pullback.hom_ext` could fire.
- **Result**: FAILED — pattern not found; the goal's structure post-simp is
  not amenable to `change`-based normalization.

### Why no helpers landed

The directive's helper budget +2 was earmarked for two named
`@[reassoc (attr := simp)]` projection lemmas:
`gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso_inv_fst` and `_inv_snd`.

Three obstacles prevent landing them this iter:

1. **RHS determination is non-trivial**: each projection lemma's RHS is the
   explicit `pullback.lift` form into `(cover).X i = pullback (PLB.fst Gm.hom)
   (openCover.f i)`. Computing each component requires tracing through the
   10-step `≪≫`-chain of the iso to determine the actual ring map at the
   intersection. Without `@[simps]` on the iso (which the analogist correctly
   notes does NOT help — its Decision 2 verdict), the components must be
   hand-derived.
2. **`pullback.hom_ext` decomposition** of each projection lemma would
   require ~50-80 LOC of fine-grained chasing of the iso's inv-projections
   through Mathlib `_inv_fst/_snd` simp lemmas. The `pullbackSpecIso` step
   is the LAST in the chain, so its `pullbackSpecIso_inv_fst/_snd` simp
   lemmas fire FIRST on the iso.inv, leaving 6 more inv-projection steps
   to chase — and as empirically verified, the chain doesn't auto-collapse.
3. **Gate budget reality**: adding the 2 projection lemmas as named typed-sorries
   (without their bodies) would add +2 to sorry count (4 → 6), regressing the
   gate. Adding them as fully-proved would require ~100-160 LOC across both
   lemmas, beyond practical single-iter budget for a CHURNING lane.

## Recommended iter-187 pickup paths (per chapter §III)

### Path (III.a) — Term-mode iso refactor [RECOMMENDED]

Refactor `gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` from its current
`refine ≪≫ ?_; refine ≪≫ ?_; ...` tactic-mode construction to a SINGLE
explicit `Iso.trans`-spine with explicit intermediate type annotations.
Estimated ~30-50 LOC; requires the `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency
false` recipe from iter-180 (which fixed `gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq`).

**Rationale for "recommended"**: the analogist's empirical Probes 2-4 in
`analogies/gmscaling-projection-idiom.md` (lines 180-223) verify that the
canonical simp set DOES collapse `(asIso (pullback.map …)).inv ≫ pullback.fst`
**in isolation**. The 5+ iters of CHURNING are due to the tactic-mode
elaboration not exposing the `Iso.trans` spine syntactically. Refactoring
this single iso to term-mode should unblock the entire chain.

**Risk**: explicit intermediate types are bulky; instance synthesis for
`HasPullback` may need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false`
to resolve.

### Path (III.b) — Named projection lemmas via `pullback.hom_ext`

Package `gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso_inv_fst/_snd` as
`@[reassoc (attr := simp)]` declarations with hand-written proofs via
`pullback.hom_ext` decomposition on the codomain (which IS a pullback).
Each RHS is a `pullback.lift` of two explicit projections computed by
tracing the iso links. Estimated ~50-80 LOC per lemma + ~10-20 LOC for
cross01 closure.

**Risk**: tracing each projection requires careful work; without
`@[simps]` annotation on the iso, each component must be hand-derived.

### Path (III.c) — Separating-sheaf alternative

Bypass the iso entirely via genus-0 separated-locus alternative; depends
on Mathlib's `IsSeparatedOver` / `Scheme.sheafExt` at commit `b80f227`.
Estimated ~80-120 LOC. This is the iter-187 backup if neither (III.a)
nor (III.b) lands.

## Other sorries — UNCHANGED this iter

### gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero (L665)

Sorry retained. The proof structure is set up via `Over.OverMorphism.ext`
+ `simp [Over.comp_left, Over.lift_left]`. The substantive step requires
`Cover.hom_ext` on `gmScalingP1_cover` to reduce to a per-chart identity,
then computing the chart-1 ring map's action on `zeroPt`'s factor through
`Spec.map (eval-at-0)`. Estimated ~30-50 LOC if approached cleanly;
deferred to iter-187+ (not in the iter-186 Lane B directive's scope).

### gm_geomIrred (L761)

Sorry retained. Blocked by the Mathlib gap
`Algebra.TensorProduct.isDomain_of_isAlgClosed_left` at commit `b80f227`
(CONFIRMED gap, iter-185 lean-vs-blueprint-checker). No path to close
without upstream Mathlib bridge or a project-side ~30-50 LOC shim via
`Function.Injective.isDomain` on the chain
`algebraMap (kbar ⊗ A) (kbar ⊗ A)`.

### projGm_isReduced (L791)

Sorry retained. Same Mathlib gap as `gm_geomIrred`.

## Notes for the plan agent

- **Gate missed**: the iter-186 sorry decrement gate (4 → ≤3 MANDATORY) was
  NOT met. The iter-186 chapter expansion was a meaningful structural advance
  in the BLUEPRINT (pinning 2 new project simp helpers + 4 product-stability
  mini-blocks + expanded `lem:gmscaling_chart_agreement` proof sketch with
  the (I)/(II)/(III.a-c)/(IV) decomposition), but the Lean-side advance
  this iter is documentation-only: the inline comment in `cross01` now
  records the empirical iter-186 simp probes and the precise residual goal
  shape.
- **iter-187 dispatch recommendation**: PROCEED WITH PATH (III.a) per the
  chapter's analysis. The expected outcome (cheapest path) makes it the
  highest-EV iter-187 lane B target. If (III.a) lands, the existing simp
  chain in cross01 should fire to closure (or near-closure). If (III.a)
  doesn't land cleanly, fall back to (III.b) or (III.c) per the recipe.
- **Failure mode trigger**: per the directive, if iter-186 misses the gate
  (which it did), iter-187 should open the genus-0 separated-locus
  alternative (Recipe III.c from the iter-186 chapter expansion). Chart-bridge
  cross-case work pauses. This is the documented mandatory pivot trigger.
- **Recipe 1 simp helpers remain available**: the iter-184 Recipe 1 helpers
  `pullback_map_fst_proj` / `pullback_map_snd_proj` (L51-69) ARE in the
  globally-active simp set and remain usable for any iter-187 lane.
- **No protected signatures touched**.

private noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso
    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    pullback ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (0 : Fin 2))
        ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (1 : Fin 2)) ≅
      Spec (CommRingCat.of
        (TensorProduct kbar
          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
            ((MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) * MvPolynomial.X 1))
          (GmRing kbar))) := by
  refine pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
